text.Rotherham: They were willing, therefore, to receive him into the boat; and, straightway, the boat was at land, whither they had been slowly going.
text.KJV: 6:21 Then they willingly receiued him into the ship, and immediatly the ship was at the land whither they went.

text.NRSV: The next day the crowd that had stayed on the other side of the sea saw that there had been only one boat there. They also saw that Jesus had not got into the boat with his disciples, but that his disciples had gone away alone.
text.Rotherham: On the morrow, the multitude that was standing on the other side of the sea, saw that there was not, another small-boat, there, save one,—and that Jesus entered not, along with his disciples, unto the boat, but that, alone, his disciples departed:—
text.KJV: 6:22 ¶ The day following, when the people which stood on the other side of the sea, saw that there was none other boat there, saue that one whereinto his disciples were entred, and that Iesus went not with his disciples into the boat, but that his disciples were gone away alone:

text.NRSV: Then some boats from Tiberias came near the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
text.Rotherham: howbeit there came boats out of Tiberias, near the place where they did eat the bread, after the Lord had given thanks:—
text.KJV: 6:23 Howbeit there came other boats from Tiberias, nigh vnto the place where they did eate bread, after that the Lord had giuen thankes:

text.NRSV: So when the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they themselves got into the boats and went to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, the multitude saw, that, Jesus, was not there, nor yet his disciples, they themselves, got into the small boats, and came unto Capernaum, seeking Jesus;
text.KJV: 6:24 When the people therefore saw that Iesus was not there, neither his disciples, they also tooke shipping, and came to Capernaum, seeking for Iesus.

text.NRSV: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they said to him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”
text.Rotherham: and, finding him on the other side of the sea, they said unto him—Rabbi! when, camest thou, hither?
text.KJV: 6:25 And when they had found him on the other side of the sea, they saide vnto him, Rabbi, when camest thou hither?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Very truly, I tell you, you are looking for me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate your fill of the loaves.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them, and said—Verily, verily, I say unto you: Ye seek me, not because ye saw signs, but because ye did eat of the loaves and were filled.
text.KJV: 6:26 Iesus answered them, and said, Uerely, verely I say vnto you, Ye seeke me, not because ye saw the miracles, but because yee did eate of the loaues, and were filled.

text.NRSV: Do not work for the food that perishes, but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you. For it is on him that God the Father has set his seal.”
text.Rotherham: Be working, not for the food that perisheth, but for the food that endureth unto life age-abiding,—which, the Son of Man, unto you, will give; for upon, the same, hath the Father, even God, set his seal.
text.KJV: 6:27 Labour not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth vnto euerlasting life, which the Sonne of man shall giue vnto you: for him hath God the Father sealed.

text.NRSV: Then they said to him, “What must we do to perform the works of God?”
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, unto him—What are we to do, that we may be working the works of God?
text.KJV: 6:28 Then said they vnto him, What shall we doe, that we might worke the workes of God?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—This, is the work of God: that ye believe on him whom, he, hath sent forth.
text.KJV: 6:29 Iesus answered, and said vnto them, This is the worke of God, that ye beleeue on him whom he hath sent.

text.NRSV: So they said to him, “What sign are you going to give us then, so that we may see it and believe you? What work are you performing?
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, unto him—What, then, art, thou, doing, by way of sign, that we may see, and believe in thee: what art thou working?
text.KJV: 6:30 They said therefore vnto him, What signe shewest thou then, that we may see, and beleeue thee? What doest thou worke?

text.NRSV: Our ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written, ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’ ”
text.Rotherham: Our fathers, did eat, the manna, in the desert,—just as it is written: Bread out of heaven, he gave them to eat.
text.KJV: 6:31 Our fathers did eate Manna in the desert, as it is written, He gaue them bread from heauen to eate.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but it is my Father who gives you the true bread from heaven.
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—Verily, verily, I say unto you: Not Moses, gave you the bread out of heaven; but, my Father, giveth you the real bread out of heaven.
text.KJV: 6:32 Then Iesus said vnto them, Uerely, verely I say vnto you, Moses gaue you not that bread from heauen, but my Father giueth you the true bread from heauen.

text.NRSV: For the bread of God is that which comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
text.Rotherham: For, the bread of God, is that which is coming down out of heaven, and giving, life, unto the world.
text.KJV: 6:33 For the bread of God is hee which commeth downe from heauen, and giueth life vnto the world.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Sir, give us this bread always.”
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, unto him—Sir! Evermore, give us this bread.
text.KJV: 6:34 Then said they vnto him, Lord, euermore giue vs this bread.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never be hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—I, am the bread of life: he that cometh unto me, in nowise shall hunger, and, he that believeth on me, in nowise shall thirst, any more.
text.KJV: 6:35 And Iesus said vnto them, I am the bread of life: hee that commeth to me, shall neuer hunger: and he that beleeueth on me, shall neuer thirst.

text.NRSV: But I said to you that you have seen me and yet do not believe.
text.Rotherham: But I told you—Ye have even seen [me], and yet do not believe.
text.KJV: 6:36 But I said vnto you, that ye also haue seene me, and beleeue not.

text.NRSV: Everything that the Father gives me will come to me, and anyone who comes to me I will never drive away;
text.Rotherham: All that which the Father is giving me, unto me, will have come, and, him that cometh unto me, in nowise will I cast out,—
text.KJV: 6:37 All that the Father giueth mee, shall come to mee; and him that commeth to me, I will in no wise cast out.

text.NRSV: for I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of him who sent me.
text.Rotherham: Because I have come down from heaven,—Not that I should be doing my own will, but the will of him that sent me.
text.KJV: 6:38 For I came downe from heauen, not to doe mine owne will, but the will of him that sent me.

text.NRSV: And this is the will of him who sent me, that I should lose nothing of all that he has given me, but raise it up on the last day.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the will of him that sent me, That, of all that which he hath given me, I should lose nothing, but should raise it up at the last day.
text.KJV: 6:39 And this is the Fathers wil which hath sent me, that of all which he hath giuen mee, I should lose nothing, but should raise it vp againe at the last day.

text.NRSV: This is indeed the will of my Father, that all who see the Son and believe in him may have eternal life; and I will raise them up on the last day.”
text.Rotherham: For, this, is the will of my Father, That, every one that vieweth the Son, and believeth on him, should have life age-abiding, and, I, should raise him up, at the last day.
text.KJV: 6:40 And this is the will of him that sent me, that euery one which seeth the Sonne, and beleeueth on him, may haue euerlasting life: and I will raise him vp at the last day.

text.NRSV: Then the Jews began to complain about him because he said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven.”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, began to murmur concerning him, because he said—I, am the bread that came down out of heaven;
text.KJV: 6:41 The Iewes then murmured at him, because hee said, I am the bread which came downe from heauen.

text.NRSV: They were saying, “Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How can he now say, ‘I have come down from heaven’?”
text.Rotherham: and were saying—Is not, this, Jesus, the son of Joseph,—of whom, we, know the father and the mother! How is it then, that he, now, saith: Out of heaven, have I come down?
text.KJV: 6:42 And they said, Is not this Iesus the sonne of Ioseph, whose father and mother we know? How is it then that hee sayth, I came downe from heauen?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Do not complain among yourselves.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—Be not murmuring, one with another:
text.KJV: 6:43 Iesus therefore answered, and said vnto them, Murmure not among your selues.

text.NRSV: No one can come to me unless drawn by the Father who sent me; and I will raise that person up on the last day.
text.Rotherham: No one, can come unto me, except, the Father who sent me, draw him,—and, I, will raise him up, in the last day.
text.KJV: 6:44 No man can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me, draw him: and I will raise him vp at the last day.

text.NRSV: It is written in the prophets, ‘And they shall all be taught by God.’ Everyone who has heard and learned from the Father comes to me.
text.Rotherham: It is written in the prophets,—And they shall be, all, the instructed of God: Every one who hath heard of the Father, and learned, cometh unto me.
text.KJV: 6:45 It is written in the Prophets, And they shall be all taught of God. Euery man therefore that hath heard, and hath learned of the Father, commeth vnto me,

text.NRSV: Not that anyone has seen the Father except the one who is from God; he has seen the Father.
text.Rotherham: Not that any one hath seen, the Father, save he who is from God,—this one, hath seen the Father.
text.KJV: 6:46 Not that any man hath seene the Father; saue hee which is of God, hee hath seene the Father.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, whoever believes has eternal life.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you: He that believeth, hath life age-abiding.
text.KJV: 6:47 Uerely, verely I say vnto you, Hee that beleeueth on me, hath euerlasting life.

text.NRSV: I am the bread of life.
text.Rotherham: I am the bread of life:—
text.KJV: 6:48 I am that bread of life.

text.NRSV: Your ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.
text.Rotherham: Your fathers, did eat, in the desert, the manna,—and died:
text.KJV: 6:49 Your fathers did eate Manna in the wildernesse, and are dead.

text.NRSV: This is the bread that comes down from heaven, so that one may eat of it and not die.
text.Rotherham: This, is the bread which, out of heaven, cometh down, that one, thereof, may eat,—and not die.
text.KJV: 6:50 This is the bread which commeth downe from heauen, that a man may eate thereof, and not die.

text.NRSV: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats of this bread will live forever; and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”
text.Rotherham: I, am the living bread, which, out of heaven, came down: If one eat of this bread, he shall live unto times age-abiding; and, the bread, moreover, which, I, will give, is, my flesh—for the world’s life.
text.KJV: 6:51 I am the liuing bread, which came downe from heauen. If any man eate of this bread, he shall liue for euer: and the bread that I will giue, is my flesh, which I will giue for the life of the world.

text.NRSV: The Jews then disputed among themselves, saying, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, began to strive one with another, saying—How can this one, unto us, give his flesh to eat?
text.KJV: 6:52 The Iewes therefore stroue amongst themselues, saying, How can this man giue vs his flesh to eate?

text.NRSV: So Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said unto them—Verily, verily, I say unto you—Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of Man, and drink his blood, ye have not life within yourselves.
text.KJV: 6:53 Then Iesus sayd vnto them, Uerely, verely I say vnto you, Except yee eate the flesh of the sonne of man, and drinke his blood, yee haue no life in you.

text.NRSV: Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood have eternal life, and I will raise them up on the last day;
text.Rotherham: He that feedeth upon my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath life age-abiding, and, I, will raise him up at the last day;
text.KJV: 6:54 Whoso eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath eternall life, and I will raise him vp at the last day.

text.NRSV: for my flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
text.Rotherham: For, my flesh, is, true, food, and, my blood, is, true, drink:
text.KJV: 6:55 For my flesh is meate indeed, and my blood is drinke indeed.

text.NRSV: Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood abide in me, and I in them.
text.Rotherham: He that feedeth upon my flesh, and drinketh my blood, in me, abideth, and, I, in him.
text.KJV: 6:56 He that eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, dwelleth in me, and I in him.

text.NRSV: Just as the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me will live because of me.
text.Rotherham: Just as the living Father sent me,—and I live by reason of the Father, he also that feedeth upon me, even he, shall live by reason of me.
text.KJV: 6:57 As the liuing Father hath sent me, and I liue by the Father: so, he that eateth me, euen he shall liue by me.

text.NRSV: This is the bread that came down from heaven, not like that which your ancestors ate, and they died. But the one who eats this bread will live forever.”
text.Rotherham: This, is the bread, which, out of heaven, came down:—Not just as your fathers did eat—and died! He that feedeth upon this bread, shall live unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 6:58 This is that bread which came downe from heauen: not as your fathers did eate Manna, and are dead: he that eateth of this bread, shall liue for euer.

text.NRSV: He said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue at Capernaum.
text.Rotherham: These things, said he, as, in a synagogue, he was teaching, in Capernaum.
text.KJV: 6:59 These things said hee in the Synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.

text.NRSV: When many of his disciples heard it, they said, “This teaching is difficult; who can accept it?”
text.Rotherham: Many, of his disciples, therefore, when they heard, said—Hard, is this discourse,—Who can, thereunto, hearken?
text.KJV: 6:60 Many therefore of his disciples, when they had heard this, said, This is an hard saying, who can heare it?

text.NRSV: But Jesus, being aware that his disciples were complaining about it, said to them, “Does this offend you?
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, knowing within himself that his disciples were murmuring concerning this, said unto them—Doth, this, cause, you, to stumble?
text.KJV: 6:61 When Iesus knew in himselfe, that his disciples murmured at it, hee said vnto them, Doeth this offend you?

text.NRSV: Then what if you were to see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?
text.Rotherham: [What] then, if ye should view the Son of Man ascending where he was before? …
text.KJV: 6:62 What and if yee shall see the sonne of man ascend vp where hee was before?

text.NRSV: It is the spirit that gives life; the flesh is useless. The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
text.Rotherham: The spirit, it is, that giveth life,—the flesh, profiteth, nothing: The declarations which, I, have spoken unto you, are, spirit, and, are, life.
text.KJV: 6:63 It is the Spirit that quickeneth, the flesh profiteth nothing: the wordes that I speake vnto you, they are Spirit, and they are life.

text.NRSV: But among you there are some who do not believe.” For Jesus knew from the first who were the ones that did not believe, and who was the one that would betray him.
text.Rotherham: But there are some from among you, who do not believe. For Jesus knew from the beginning, who they were that did not believe, and who it was would deliver him up;—
text.KJV: 6:64 But there are some of you that beleeue not. For Iesus knew from the beginning, who they were that beleeued not, and who should betray him.

text.NRSV: And he said, “For this reason I have told you that no one can come to me unless it is granted by the Father.”
text.Rotherham: and he went on to say—For this cause, have I said unto you: No one, can come unto me, except it have been given him, of the Father.
text.KJV: 6:65 And he said, Therefore said I vnto you, that no man can come vnto me, except it were giuen vnto him of my Father.

text.NRSV: Because of this many of his disciples turned back and no longer went about with him.
text.Rotherham: Because of this, many from among his disciples, went away back, and, no longer, with him, were walking.
text.KJV: 6:66 ¶ From that time many of his disciples went backe, and walked no more with him.

text.NRSV: So Jesus asked the twelve, “Do you also wish to go away?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said unto the twelve—Are, ye also, wishing to withdraw?
text.KJV: 6:67 Then said Iesus vnto the twelue, Will ye also goe away?

text.NRSV: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom can we go? You have the words of eternal life.
text.Rotherham: Simon Peter answered him—Lord! unto whom, shall we go? Declarations of life age-abiding, thou hast;
text.KJV: 6:68 Then Simon Peter answered him, Lord, to whom shall we goe? Thou hast the words of eternall life.

text.NRSV: We have come to believe and know that you are the Holy One of God.”
text.Rotherham: And, we, have believed, and come to know,—that, thou, art the Holy One of God.
text.KJV: 6:69 And we beleeue and are sure that thou art that Christ, the Sonne of the liuing God.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Did I not choose you, the twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them—Did not, I, make choice, of you, the twelve? And yet, from among you, one, is, an adversary.
text.KJV: 6:70 Iesus answered them, Haue not I chosen you twelue, and one of you is a deuill?

text.NRSV: He was speaking of Judas son of Simon Iscariot, for he, though one of the twelve, was going to betray him.
text.Rotherham: Now he was speaking of Judas, son of Simon Iscariot: for, the same, was about to deliver him up,—one of the twelve.
text.KJV: 6:71 He spake of Iudas Iscariot the sonne of Simon: for hee it was that should betray him, being one of the twelue.

text.NRSV: After this Jesus went about in Galilee. He did not wish to go about in Judea because the Jews were looking for an opportunity to kill him.
text.Rotherham: And, after these things, Jesus was walking in Galilee; for he was not wishing, in Judaea, to be walking, because the Jews were seeking to slay him.
text.KJV: 7:1 After these things, Iesus walked in Galilee: for hee would not walk in Iurie, because the Iewes sought to kill him.

text.NRSV: Now the Jewish festival of Booths was near.
text.Rotherham: But the feast of the Jews was near, that of the pitching of tents.
text.KJV: 7:2 Now the Iewes feast of Tabernacles was at hand.

text.NRSV: So his brothers said to him, “Leave here and go to Judea so that your disciples also may see the works you are doing;
text.Rotherham: His brethren, therefore, said unto him—Remove hence, and withdraw into Judaea, that, thy disciples also, may view thy works which thou art doing.
text.KJV: 7:3 His brethren therefore saide vnto him, Depart hence, and go into Iudea, that thy Disciples also may see the works that thou doest.

text.NRSV: for no one who wants to be widely known acts in secret. If you do these things, show yourself to the world.”
text.Rotherham: For, no one, anything in secret, doeth, and yet is seeking, himself, to be well-known. If, these things, thou art doing, manifest thyself unto the world.
text.KJV: 7:4 For there is no man that doth any thing in secret, and hee himselfe seeketh to be knowen openly: If thou doe these things, shew thy selfe to þ world.

text.NRSV: (For not even his brothers believed in him.)
text.Rotherham: For, not even his brethren, were believing on him.
text.KJV: 7:5 For neither did his brethren beleeue in him.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “My time has not yet come, but your time is always here.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, saith unto them—My season, not yet, is here; but, your season, at anytime, is ready:
text.KJV: 7:6 Then Iesus said vnto them, My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready.

text.NRSV: The world cannot hate you, but it hates me because I testify against it that its works are evil.
text.Rotherham: The world cannot hate you; but, me, it hateth, because, I, bear witness concerning it, that, the works thereof, are, wicked.
text.KJV: 7:7 The world cannot hate you, but me it hateth, because I testifie of it, that the workes thereof are euill.

text.NRSV: Go to the festival yourselves. I am not going to this festival, for my time has not yet fully come.”
text.Rotherham: Ye, go up unto the feast: I, not yet, am coming up unto this feast, because, my season, not yet, hath fully come.
text.KJV: 7:8 Goe ye vp vnto this feast: I goe not vp yet vnto this feast, for my time is not yet full come.

text.NRSV: After saying this, he remained in Galilee.
text.Rotherham: And, these things, having said unto them, he was still abiding in Galilee.
text.KJV: 7:9 When he had said these words vnto them, he abode still in Galilee.

text.NRSV: But after his brothers had gone to the festival, then he also went, not publicly but as it were in secret.
text.Rotherham: But, when his brethren had gone up unto the feast, then, he also, went up,—not openly, but as it were in secret.
text.KJV: 7:10 ¶ But when his brethren were gone vp, then went he also vp vnto the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret.

text.NRSV: The Jews were looking for him at the festival and saying, “Where is he?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, were seeking him in the feast, and were saying—Where is that one?
text.KJV: 7:11 Then the Iewes sought him at the feast, and said, Where is he?

text.NRSV: And there was considerable complaining about him among the crowds. While some said, “He is a good man,” others said, “No, he is deceiving the crowd.”
text.Rotherham: and there was great, murmuring, about him among the multitudes: some, indeed, were saying—Good, is he! [but], others, were saying—Not so! but he is leading the multitude astray.
text.KJV: 7:12 And there was much murmuring among the people, concerning him: For some said, Hee is a good man: Others said, Nay, but he deceiueth the people.

text.NRSV: Yet no one would speak openly about him for fear of the Jews.
text.Rotherham: No one, nevertheless, was speaking, openly, about him, because of their fear of the Jews.
text.KJV: 7:13 Howbeit, no man spake openly of him, for feare of the Iewes.

text.NRSV: About the middle of the festival Jesus went up into the temple and began to teach.
text.Rotherham: Presently, however, the feast being at its height, Jesus went up unto the temple and was teaching.
text.KJV: 7:14 ¶ Now about the middest of the feast, Iesus went vp into the Temple, and taught.

text.NRSV: The Jews were astonished at it, saying, “How does this man have such learning, when he has never been taught?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, began to marvel, saying—How, doth this one know, letters, not having learned?
text.KJV: 7:15 And the Iewes marueiled, saying, How knoweth this man letters, hauing neuer learned?

text.NRSV: Then Jesus answered them, “My teaching is not mine but his who sent me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, answered them, and said—My teaching, is not mine, but his who sent me.
text.KJV: 7:16 Iesus answered them, My doctrine is not mine, but his that sent me.

text.NRSV: Anyone who resolves to do the will of God will know whether the teaching is from God or whether I am speaking on my own.
text.Rotherham: If any one intend, his will, to do, he shall get to know concerning the teaching—whether it is, of God, or, I, from myself, am speaking,
text.KJV: 7:17 If any man will doe his will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speake of my selfe.

text.NRSV: Those who speak on their own seek their own glory; but the one who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true, and there is nothing false in him.
text.Rotherham: He that, from himself, doth speak, his own glory, is seeking: he that seeketh the glory of him that sent him, the same, is, true, and, injustice, in him, is there none.
text.KJV: 7:18 He that speaketh of himselfe, seeketh his owne glory: but he that seeketh his glory that sent him, the same is true, and no vnrighteousnesse is in him.

text.NRSV: “Did not Moses give you the law? Yet none of you keeps the law. Why are you looking for an opportunity to kill me?”
text.Rotherham: Did not, Moses, give you the law? And yet, not one of you, doeth the law!—Why, seek ye, to slay, me?
text.KJV: 7:19 Did not Moses giue you the Law, and yet none of you keepeth the Law? Why goe ye about to kill me?

text.NRSV: The crowd answered, “You have a demon! Who is trying to kill you?”
text.Rotherham: The multitude answered—A demon, thou hast: Who, is seeking, to slay, thee?
text.KJV: 7:20 The people answered, and sayd, Thou hast a deuill: Who goeth about to kill thee?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “I performed one work, and all of you are astonished.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—One work, I did, and ye, all, are marvelling:
text.KJV: 7:21 Iesus answered, and saide vnto them, I haue done one worke, and yee all marueile.

text.NRSV: Moses gave you circumcision (it is, of course, not from Moses, but from the patriarchs), and you circumcise a man on the sabbath.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, Moses, hath given you, circumcision,—not that, of Moses, it is, but of the fathers;—and, on Sabbath, ye circumcise a man.
text.KJV: 7:22 Moses therefore gaue vnto you Circumcision (not because it is of Moses, but of the fathers) and yee on the Sabbath day circumcise a man.

text.NRSV: If a man receives circumcision on the sabbath in order that the law of Moses may not be broken, are you angry with me because I healed a man’s whole body on the sabbath?
text.Rotherham: If, circumcision, a man receiveth, on Sabbath, that the law of Moses may not be broken, with me, are ye bitter as gall, because, a whole man, I made, well, on Sabbath?
text.KJV: 7:23 If a man on the Sabbath day receiue circumcision, that the Lawe of Moses should not be broken; are ye angry at me, because I haue made a man euery whit whole on the Sabbath day?

text.NRSV: Do not judge by appearances, but judge with right judgment.”
text.Rotherham: Be not judging according to appearance, but, just judgment, be judging,
text.KJV: 7:24 Iudge not according to the appearance, but iudge righteous iudgement.

text.NRSV: Now some of the people of Jerusalem were saying, “Is not this the man they are trying to kill?
text.Rotherham: So then, some of them of Jerusalem were saying—Is not, this, he whom they are seeking to kill?
text.KJV: 7:25 Then said some of them of Hierusalem, Is not this hee, whome they seeke to kill?

text.NRSV: And here he is, speaking openly, but they say nothing to him! Can it be that the authorities really know that this is the Messiah?
text.Rotherham: And yet, see! with freedom of speech, he is talking, and, nothing, unto him, do they say:—Have the rulers perhaps come to know, of a truth, that, this, is, the Christ?
text.KJV: 7:26 But loe, he speaketh boldly, and they say nothing vnto him: Doe the rulers know indeede that this is the very Christ?

text.NRSV: Yet we know where this man is from; but when the Messiah comes, no one will know where he is from.”
text.Rotherham: But, as for this one, we know whence he is; The Christ, however, whensoever he shall come, no one, getteth to know whence he is.
text.KJV: 7:27 Howbeit wee know this man whence he is: but when Christ commeth, no man knoweth whence he is.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus cried out as he was teaching in the temple, “You know me, and you know where I am from. I have not come on my own. But the one who sent me is true, and you do not know him.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, cried aloud in the temple, teaching, and saying—Both me, ye know, and ye know whence I am,—and yet, of myself, have I not come, but he is real, who sent me, whom, ye, know not.
text.KJV: 7:28 Then cried Iesus in the Temple as he taught, saying, Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am, and I am not come of my selfe, but he that sent me, is true, whom ye know not.

text.NRSV: I know him, because I am from him, and he sent me.”
text.Rotherham: I, know him, because, from him, I am, and, he, sent me forth.
text.KJV: 7:29 But I know him, for I am from him, and he hath sent me.

text.NRSV: Then they tried to arrest him, but no one laid hands on him, because his hour had not yet come.
text.Rotherham: They were seeking therefore to seize him; and yet no one laid on him his hand, because, not yet, had come his hour.
text.KJV: 7:30 Then they sought to take him: but no man laid hands on him, because his houre was not yet come.

text.NRSV: Yet many in the crowd believed in him and were saying, “When the Messiah comes, will he do more signs than this man has done?”
text.Rotherham: But, from amongst the multitude, many, believed on him, and were saying—The Christ, whensoever he cometh, greater signs, will he do, than those which, this one, did?
text.KJV: 7:31 And many of the people beleeued on him, & said, When Christ commeth, will hee doe moe miracles then these which this man hath done?

text.NRSV: The Pharisees heard the crowd muttering such things about him, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent temple police to arrest him.
text.Rotherham: The Pharisees hearkened unto the multitude murmuring concerning him these things; and the High-priests and the Pharisees sent forth officers, that they might seize him.
text.KJV: 7:32 ¶ The Pharisees heard that the people murmured such things concerning him: And the Pharisees and the chiefe Priests sent officers to take him.

text.NRSV: Jesus then said, “I will be with you a little while longer, and then I am going to him who sent me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said—Yet a little time, I am, with you, and withdraw unto him that sent me:
text.KJV: 7:33 Then said Iesus vnto them, Yet a litle while am I with you, and then I goe vnto him that sent me.

text.NRSV: You will search for me, but you will not find me; and where I am, you cannot come.”
text.Rotherham: Ye shall seek me, and not find me, and, where, I, am, ye, cannot come.
text.KJV: 7:34 Ye shall seeke me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither yee cannot come.

text.NRSV: The Jews said to one another, “Where does this man intend to go that we will not find him? Does he intend to go to the Dispersion among the Greeks and teach the Greeks?
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, said unto themselves—Whither, is, this one, about to go, that we shall not find him? Unto the dispersion of the Greeks, is he about to go, and teach the Greeks?
text.KJV: 7:35 Then saide the Iewes among themselues, Whither will hee goe, that we shall not find him? will he goe vnto the dispersed among the Gentiles, and teach the Gentiles?

text.NRSV: What does he mean by saying, ‘You will search for me and you will not find me’ and ‘Where I am, you cannot come’?”
text.Rotherham: What, is this which he said: Ye shall seek me and not find me, and, where, I, am, ye, cannot come?
text.KJV: 7:36 What maner of saying is this that he said, Ye shall seeke me, and shall not find me? and where I am, thither ye cannot come?

text.NRSV: On the last day of the festival, the great day, while Jesus was standing there, he cried out, “Let anyone who is thirsty come to me,
text.Rotherham: Now, on the last—the great—day of the feast, Jesus was standing, and he cried aloud, saying—If any man thirst, let him come unto me and drink:
text.KJV: 7:37 In the last day, that great day of the feast, Iesus stood, and cried, saying, If any man thirst, let him come vnto me, and drinke.

text.NRSV: and let the one who believes in me drink. As the scripture has said, ‘Out of the believer’s heart shall flow rivers of living water.’ ”
text.Rotherham: He that believeth on me—just as said the Scripture,—River, from within him, shall flow, of living water.
text.KJV: 7:38 He that beleeueth on me, as the Scripture hath saide, out of his belly shall flow riuers of liuing water.

text.NRSV: Now he said this about the Spirit, which believers in him were to receive; for as yet there was no Spirit, because Jesus was not yet glorified.
text.Rotherham: Now, this, spake he concerning the Spirit which they who believed on him were about to receive; for, not yet, was there Spirit, because, Jesus, not yet, was glorified!
text.KJV: 7:39 (But this spake he of the Spirit which they that beleeue on him, should receiue. For the holy Ghost was not yet giuen, because that Iesus was not yet glorified.)

text.NRSV: When they heard these words, some in the crowd said, “This is really the prophet.”
text.Rotherham: Some from amongst the multitude, therefore, having hearkened unto these words, were saying—This, is, in truth, the prophet.
text.KJV: 7:40 ¶ Many of the people therefore, when they heard this saying, saide, Of a trueth this is the Prophet.

text.NRSV: Others said, “This is the Messiah.” But some asked, “Surely the Messiah does not come from Galilee, does he?
text.Rotherham: Others, were saying—This, is, the Christ. But, others, were saying—Out of Galilee, is, the Christ, to come?
text.KJV: 7:41 Others said, This is the Christ. But some said, Shall Christ come out of Galilee?

text.NRSV: Has not the scripture said that the Messiah is descended from David and comes from Bethlehem, the village where David lived?”
text.Rotherham: Hath not the Scripture said: Of the seed of David, and from Bethlehem, the village where David was, cometh the Christ?
text.KJV: 7:42 Hath not the Scripture saide, that Christ commeth of the seede of Dauid, and out of the towne of Bethlehem, where Dauid was?

text.NRSV: So there was a division in the crowd because of him.
text.Rotherham: A division, therefore, arose in the multitude, because of him.
text.KJV: 7:43 So there was diuision among the people because of him.

text.NRSV: Some of them wanted to arrest him, but no one laid hands on him.
text.Rotherham: Certain, indeed, from among them, were wishing to seize him; but, no one, thrust upon him his hands.
text.KJV: 7:44 And some of them would haue taken him, but no man layed hands on him.

text.NRSV: Then the temple police went back to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked them, “Why did you not arrest him?”
text.Rotherham: So then the officers came unto the High-priests and Pharisees, and, they, said unto them—For what cause, have ye not brought him?
text.KJV: 7:45 ¶ Then came the officers to the chiefe Priests and Pharises, and they said vnto them, Why haue ye not brought him?

text.NRSV: The police answered, “Never has anyone spoken like this!”
text.Rotherham: The officers answered—Never, man spake thus!
text.KJV: 7:46 The officers answered, Neuer man spake like this man.

text.NRSV: Then the Pharisees replied, “Surely you have not been deceived too, have you?
text.Rotherham: The Pharisees, therefore, answered them—Surely, ye also, have not been led astray?
text.KJV: 7:47 Then answered them the Pharisees, Are ye also deceiued?

text.NRSV: Has any one of the authorities or of the Pharisees believed in him?
text.Rotherham: Surely, none of the rulers, hath believed in him, nor of the Pharisees?
text.KJV: 7:48 Haue any of the rulers, or of the Pharises beleeued on him?

text.NRSV: But this crowd, which does not know the law—they are accursed.”
text.Rotherham: But, this multitude, that take no note of the law, are, laid under a curse.
text.KJV: 7:49 But this people who knoweth not the Law, are cursed.

text.NRSV: Nicodemus, who had gone to Jesus before, and who was one of them, asked,
text.Rotherham: Nicodemus saith unto them—he that came unto him formerly, being, one, from among them—
text.KJV: 7:50 Nicodemus saith vnto them, (He that came to Iesus by night, being one of them,)

text.NRSV: “Our law does not judge people without first giving them a hearing to find out what they are doing, does it?”
text.Rotherham: Surely, our law, doth not judge the man, unless it hear first from him, and get to know what he is doing?
text.KJV: 7:51 Doth our Law iudge any man before it heare him, & know what he doth?

text.NRSV: They replied, “Surely you are not also from Galilee, are you? Search and you will see that no prophet is to arise from Galilee.”
text.Rotherham: They answered and said unto him—Surely, thou also, art not, of Galilee? Search and see! That, out of Galilee, a prophet is not to arise.
text.KJV: 7:52 They answered, and said vnto him, Art thou also of Galilee? Search, and looke: for out of Galilee ariseth no Prophet.

text.NRSV: Then each of them went home,
text.Rotherham: [[And they went, each unto his own house;
text.KJV: 7:53 And euery man went vnto his owne house.

text.NRSV: while Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
text.Rotherham: but, Jesus, went unto the Mount of Olives.
text.KJV: 8:1 Iesus went vnto þ Mount of Oliues:

text.NRSV: Early in the morning he came again to the temple. All the people came to him and he sat down and began to teach them.
text.Rotherham: And, early in the morning, again, came he into the temple, and all the people were coming unto him; and, sitting down, he began to teach them.
text.KJV: 8:2 And earely in the morning hee came againe into the Temple, and all the people came vnto him, and he sate downe, and taught them.

text.NRSV: The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman who had been caught in adultery; and making her stand before all of them,
text.Rotherham: And the Scribes and the Pharisees bring a woman, caught, in adultery; and, setting her in the midst,
text.KJV: 8:3 And the Scribes and Pharisees brought vnto him a woman taken in adultery, and when they had set her in the mids,

text.NRSV: they said to him, “Teacher, this woman was caught in the very act of committing adultery.
text.Rotherham: they say unto him—Teacher! this woman, hath been caught in the very act of committing adultery!
text.KJV: 8:4 They say vnto him, Master, this woman was taken in adultery, in the very act.

text.NRSV: Now in the law Moses commanded us to stone such women. Now what do you say?”
text.Rotherham: Now, in the law, Moses, [unto us] gave command to stone, such as these. What, then, dost, thou, say?
text.KJV: 8:5 Now Moses in the Law commanded vs, that such should be stoned: but what sayest thou?

text.NRSV: They said this to test him, so that they might have some charge to bring against him. Jesus bent down and wrote with his finger on the ground.
text.Rotherham: [This, however, they were saying, by way of testing him,—that they might have whereof to accuse him.] But, Jesus, stooping down, with his finger, wrote in the ground.
text.KJV: 8:6 This they said, tempting him, that they might haue to accuse him. But Iesus stouped downe, and with his finger wrote on the ground as though he heard them not.

text.NRSV: When they kept on questioning him, he straightened up and said to them, “Let anyone among you who is without sin be the first to throw a stone at her.”
text.Rotherham: When, however, they still continued questioning him, he lifted himself up and said [unto them]—He of you, that is without sin, let him first cast at her a stone;
text.KJV: 8:7 So when they continued asking him, hee lift vp himselfe, and saide vnto them, Hee that is without sinne among you, let him first cast a stone at her.

text.NRSV: And once again he bent down and wrote on the ground.
text.Rotherham: and, again stooping down, he wrote in the ground.
text.KJV: 8:8 And againe, hee stouped downe, and wrote on the ground.

text.NRSV: When they heard it, they went away, one by one, beginning with the elders; and Jesus was left alone with the woman standing before him.
text.Rotherham: And, they who heard, began to go out, one by one, beginning from the elders,—and he was left, alone; the woman also, being, in the midst.
text.KJV: 8:9 And they which heard it, being conuicted by their owne conscience, went out one by one, beginning at the eldest, euen vnto the last: and Iesus was left alone, and the woman standing in the midst.

text.NRSV: Jesus straightened up and said to her, “Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?”
text.Rotherham: And lifting himself up, Jesus said unto her—Woman, where are they? Hath, no one, condemned thee?
text.KJV: 8:10 When Iesus had lift vp himselfe, and saw none but the woman, hee said vnto her, Woman, where are those thine accusers? Hath no man condemned thee?

text.NRSV: She said, “No one, sir.” And Jesus said, “Neither do I condemn you. Go your way, and from now on do not sin again.”
text.Rotherham: And, she, said—No one, Sir! And Jesus said—Neither do, I, condemn thee,—be going thy way: Henceforth, be sinning, no more.]]
text.KJV: 8:11 She saide, No man, Lord. And Iesus saide vnto her, Neither doe I condemne thee: Goe, and sinne no more.

text.NRSV: Again Jesus spoke to them, saying, “I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness but will have the light of life.”
text.Rotherham: Again, therefore, unto them, spake Jesus, saying—I, am the light of the world: He that followeth me, shall in nowise walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.
text.KJV: 8:12 ¶ Then spake Iesus againe vnto them, saying, I am the light of the world: he that followeth mee, shall not walke in darkenesse, but shall haue the light of life.

text.NRSV: Then the Pharisees said to him, “You are testifying on your own behalf; your testimony is not valid.”
text.Rotherham: The Pharisees, therefore, said unto him—Thou, concerning thyself, bearest witness: thy witness, is not true.
text.KJV: 8:13 The Pharisees therefore said vnto him, Thou bearest record of thy selfe, thy record is not true.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Even if I testify on my own behalf, my testimony is valid because I know where I have come from and where I am going, but you do not know where I come from or where I am going.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered, and said unto them—Even though, I, bear witness concerning myself, true, is my witness, because I know whence I came, and whither I go; but, ye, know not whence I come, and whither I go.
text.KJV: 8:14 Iesus answered, and said vnto them, Though I beare record of my selfe, yet my record is true: for I know whence I came, and whither I goe: but ye cannot tell whence I come, and whither I goe.

text.NRSV: You judge by human standards; I judge no one.
text.Rotherham: Ye, according to the flesh, do judge: I, am judging no one.
text.KJV: 8:15 Yee iudge after the flesh, I iudge no man.

text.NRSV: Yet even if I do judge, my judgment is valid; for it is not I alone who judge, but I and the Father who sent me.
text.Rotherham: And, even if, I, am judging, my judging, is, genuine,—because, alone, am I not, but, I, and the Father who sent me;
text.KJV: 8:16 And yet if I iudge, my iudgement is true: for I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent me.

text.NRSV: In your law it is written that the testimony of two witnesses is valid.
text.Rotherham: And, in your own law, it is written, that, two men’s witness, is, true:
text.KJV: 8:17 It is also written in your Law, that the testimonie of two men is true.

text.NRSV: I testify on my own behalf, and the Father who sent me testifies on my behalf.”
text.Rotherham: I, am the one bearing witness of myself, and the Father who sent me is bearing witness concerning me.
text.KJV: 8:18 I am one that beare witnesse of my selfe, and the Father that sent mee, beareth witnesse of me.

text.NRSV: Then they said to him, “Where is your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.”
text.Rotherham: They were saying unto him, therefore—Where, is thy father? Jesus answered—Neither, me, do ye know, nor yet my Father: If, me, ye had known, my Father also, had ye known.
text.KJV: 8:19 Then said they vnto him, Where is thy Father? Iesus answered, Ye neither know me, nor my Father: if ye had knowen mee, yee should haue knowen my Father also.

text.NRSV: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the treasury of the temple, but no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.
text.Rotherham: These sayings, spake he in the treasury, teaching in the temple, and yet, no one, seized him, because, not yet, had come his hour.
text.KJV: 8:20 These words spake Iesus in the treasury, as hee taught in the Temple: and no man layd hands on him, for his houre was not yet come.

text.NRSV: Again he said to them, “I am going away, and you will search for me, but you will die in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come.”
text.Rotherham: He said unto them again, therefore—I, go, and ye shall seek me,—and yet, in your sin, shall ye die: Whither, I, go, ye, cannot come.
text.KJV: 8:21 Then saide Iesus againe vnto them, I goe my way, and ye shall seeke me, & shall die in your sinnes: Whither I goe, ye cannot come.

text.NRSV: Then the Jews said, “Is he going to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying, ‘Where I am going, you cannot come’?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, were saying—Can it be that he will kill himself, that he saith, Whither, I, go, ye, cannot come?
text.KJV: 8:22 Then said the Iewes, Will hee kill himselfe? because he saith, Whither I goe, ye cannot come.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “You are from below, I am from above; you are of this world, I am not of this world.
text.Rotherham: And he was saying unto them—Ye, are, of the realms below, I, am, of the realms above: Ye, are, of this world, I, am not of this world.
text.KJV: 8:23 And hee said vnto them, Yee are from beneath, I am from aboue: Yee are of this world, I am not of this world.

text.NRSV: I told you that you would die in your sins, for you will die in your sins unless you believe that I am he.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore said I unto you, Ye shall die in your sins: For, if ye believe not that, I, am he, ye shall die in your sins.
text.KJV: 8:24 I said therefore vnto you, that ye shall die in your sinnes. For if yee beleeue not that I am hee, yee shall die in your sinnes.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Who are you?” Jesus said to them, “Why do I speak to you at all?
text.Rotherham: They were saying unto him, therefore—Who art, thou? Jesus said unto them—First and foremost, even what I speak unto you.
text.KJV: 8:25 Then said they vnto him, Who art thou? And Iesus saith vnto them, Euen the same that I saide vnto you from the beginning.

text.NRSV: I have much to say about you and much to condemn; but the one who sent me is true, and I declare to the world what I have heard from him.”
text.Rotherham: Many things, have I, concerning you, to speak, and to judge; but, he who sent me, is, true, and, I, what things I heard from him, the same, speak I unto the world.
text.KJV: 8:26 I haue many things to say, and to iudge of you: But hee that sent mee is true, and I speake to the world, those things which I haue heard of him.

text.NRSV: They did not understand that he was speaking to them about the Father.
text.Rotherham: They noted not that, as touching the Father, unto them, he was speaking.
text.KJV: 8:27 They vnderstood not that hee spake to them of the Father.

text.NRSV: So Jesus said, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will realize that I am he, and that I do nothing on my own, but I speak these things as the Father instructed me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said—Whensoever ye shall lift up the Son of Man, then, shall ye know, that, I, am he, and, of myself, am doing, nothing; but, just as the Father taught me, the same things, am I speaking.
text.KJV: 8:28 Then saide Iesus vnto them, When yee haue lift vp the Sonne of man, then shall ye know that I am he, and that I doe nothing of my selfe: but as my Father hath taught mee, I speake these things.

text.NRSV: And the one who sent me is with me; he has not left me alone, for I always do what is pleasing to him.”
text.Rotherham: And, he that sent me, is, with me: He hath not left me, alone. Because, I, the things that please him, ever do.
text.KJV: 8:29 And he that sent me, is with me: the Father hath not left mee alone: for I doe alwayes those things that please him.

text.NRSV: As he was saying these things, many believed in him.
text.Rotherham: As he was speaking these things, many, believed on him.
text.KJV: 8:30 As hee spake those words, many beleeued on him.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to the Jews who had believed in him, “If you continue in my word, you are truly my disciples;
text.Rotherham: Jesus was saying, therefore, unto the Jews who had believed on him—If, ye, abide in my word, of a truth, my disciples, ye are;
text.KJV: 8:31 Then said Iesus to those Iewes which beleeued on him, If ye continue in my word, then are yee my disciples indeed.

text.NRSV: and you will know the truth, and the truth will make you free.”
text.Rotherham: And ye shall know the truth, and, the truth, shall make you free.
text.KJV: 8:32 And ye shall know the Trueth, and the Trueth shall make you free.

text.NRSV: They answered him, “We are descendants of Abraham and have never been slaves to anyone. What do you mean by saying, ‘You will be made free’?”
text.Rotherham: They made answer unto him—Seed of Abraham, are we, and, unto no one, have been brought into bondage, at any time: How sayest, thou, Free, shall ye be made?
text.KJV: 8:33 ¶ They answered him, We be Abraham seed, and were neuer in bondage to any man: how sayest thou, Yee shall be made free?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Very truly, I tell you, everyone who commits sin is a slave to sin.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them, Verily, verily, I say unto you: Every one who committeth sin, is, a slave, [of sin:]
text.KJV: 8:34 Iesus answered them, Uerily, verily I say vnto you, Whosoeuer committeth sinne, is the seruant of sinne.

text.NRSV: The slave does not have a permanent place in the household; the son has a place there forever.
text.Rotherham: Now, the slave, doth not abide in the house, evermore. The Son, abideth, evermore.
text.KJV: 8:35 And the seruant abideth not in the house for euer: but the Sonne abideth euer.

text.NRSV: So if the Son makes you free, you will be free indeed.
text.Rotherham: If then, the Son, shall make you free, really free, shall ye be.
text.KJV: 8:36 If the Sonne therfore shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed.

text.NRSV: I know that you are descendants of Abraham; yet you look for an opportunity to kill me, because there is no place in you for my word.
text.Rotherham: I know that ye are, seed of Abraham; but ye are seeking to kill me, because, my word, findeth no place in you.
text.KJV: 8:37 I know that yee are Abrahams seed, but ye seeke to kill mee, because my word hath no place in you.

text.NRSV: I declare what I have seen in the Father’s presence; as for you, you should do what you have heard from your father.”
text.Rotherham: What things, I, have seen with the Father, I am speaking; Ye, also, then, what things ye have heard from your father, are doing.—
text.KJV: 8:38 I speake that which I haue seene with my Father: and ye do that which ye haue seene with your father.

text.NRSV: They answered him, “Abraham is our father.” Jesus said to them, “If you were Abraham’s children, you would be doing what Abraham did,
text.Rotherham: They answered and said unto him—Our father, is, Abraham. Jesus saith unto them—If, children of Abraham, ye are, the works of Abraham, are ye doing.
text.KJV: 8:39 They answered, and said vnto him, Abraham is our father. Iesus sayth vnto them, If yee were Abrahams children, ye would doe the works of Abraham.

text.NRSV: but now you are trying to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. This is not what Abraham did.
text.Rotherham: But, now, ye are seeking, to kill me,—A man who, the truth, unto you, hath spoken, which I have heard from God: this, Abraham, did not.
text.KJV: 8:40 But now yee seeke to kill me, a man that hath tolde you the trueth, which I haue heard of God: this did not Abraham.

text.NRSV: You are indeed doing what your father does.” They said to him, “We are not illegitimate children; we have one father, God himself.”
text.Rotherham: Ye, are doing the works of your father. They said unto him—We, of fornication, were not born: One father, have we—God.
text.KJV: 8:41 Ye doe the deeds of your father. Then said they to him, We be not borne of fornication, wee haue one Father, euen God.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God and now I am here. I did not come on my own, but he sent me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—If, God, had been your father, ye had been loving me, for, I, from God, came forth, and am here; for, not even of myself, have I come, but, he, sent me forth.
text.KJV: 8:42 Iesus said vnto them, If God were your Father, yee would loue me, for I proceeded foorth, and came from God: neither came I of my selfe, but he sent me.

text.NRSV: Why do you not understand what I say? It is because you cannot accept my word.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, is it, that, my speech, ye do not understand? because ye cannot hear my word.
text.KJV: 8:43 Why doe yee not vnderstand my speech? euen because yee cannot heare my word.

text.NRSV: You are of your father the devil, and your will is to do your father’s desires. He was a murderer from the beginning and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks according to his own nature, for he is a liar and the father of lies.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are, of your father—the adversary, and, the covetings of your father, ye choose to be doing. He, was, a murderer, from the beginning, and, in the truth, he stood not; because truth is not in him: Whensoever he speaketh falsehood, of his own, he speaketh; because, false, he is, and, the father of it.
text.KJV: 8:44 Ye are of your father the deuill, and the lusts of your father ye will doe: hee was a murtherer from the beginning, and abode not in the trueth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his owne: for he is a liar, and the father of it.

text.NRSV: But because I tell the truth, you do not believe me.
text.Rotherham: But, as for me, because, the truth, I speak, ye do not believe me.
text.KJV: 8:45 And because I tell you the truth, ye beleeue me not.

text.NRSV: Which of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?
text.Rotherham: Which of you convicteth me of sin? If, truth, I speak, wherefore, do, ye, not believe me?
text.KJV: 8:46 Which of you conuinceth mee of sinne? And if I say the trueth, why doe ye not beleeue me?

text.NRSV: Whoever is from God hears the words of God. The reason you do not hear them is that you are not from God.”
text.Rotherham: He that is of God, heareth, the sayings of God; therefore, do, ye, not hear, because, of God, ye are not.
text.KJV: 8:47 He that is of God, heareth Gods words: ye therefore heare them not, because ye are not of God.

text.NRSV: The Jews answered him, “Are we not right in saying that you are a Samaritan and have a demon?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews answered, and said unto him—Do, we, not, well, say: Thou, art, a Samaritan, and hast a demon?
text.KJV: 8:48 Then answered the Iewes, and said vnto him, Say wee not well that thou art a Samaritane, & hast a deuill?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “I do not have a demon; but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—I, have not, a demon, but honour my Father: and, ye, dishonour me.
text.KJV: 8:49 Iesus answered, I haue not a deuill: but I honour my Father, and ye doe dishonour me.

text.NRSV: Yet I do not seek my own glory; there is one who seeks it and he is the judge.
text.Rotherham: But, I, seek not my glory: There is one who seeketh and judgeth.
text.KJV: 8:50 And I seeke not mine owne glory, there is one that seeketh & iudgeth.

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, whoever keeps my word will never see death.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you: If anyone shall keep, my word, death, shall he not see, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 8:51 Uerely, verely I say vnto you, If a man keepe my saying, hee shall neuer see death.

text.NRSV: The Jews said to him, “Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham died, and so did the prophets; yet you say, ‘Whoever keeps my word will never taste death.’
text.Rotherham: The Jews said unto him—Now, we know that, a demon, thou hast:—Abraham, died, and, the prophets, and yet, thou, sayest: If anyone shall keep, my word, in nowise shall he taste of death, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 8:52 Then said the Iewes vnto him, Now we know that thou hast a deuill. Abraham is dead, and the Prophets: and thou sayest, If a man keepe my saying, he shall neuer taste of death.

text.NRSV: Are you greater than our father Abraham, who died? The prophets also died. Who do you claim to be?”
text.Rotherham: Surely, thou, art not, greater, than our father Abraham,—who, indeed, died? and, the prophets, died:—Whom, makest thou thyself?
text.KJV: 8:53 Art thou greater then our father Abraham, which is dead? and the Prophets are dead: whom makest thou thy selfe?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, he of whom you say, ‘He is our God,’
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—If, I, glorify myself, my glory, is nothing: It is, my Father, that glorifieth me,—of whom, ye, say—He is, your God!
text.KJV: 8:54 Iesus answered, If I honour myselfe, my honour is nothing: it is my Father that honoureth me, of whom ye say, that he is your God:

text.NRSV: though you do not know him. But I know him; if I would say that I do not know him, I would be a liar like you. But I do know him and I keep his word.
text.Rotherham: And yet ye have not come to know him; but, I, do know him: If I say, I know him not, I shall be like you—false; but I know him, and, his word, am I keeping.
text.KJV: 8:55 Yet ye haue not knowen him, but I know him: and if I should say, I know him not, I shalbe a lyar like vnto you: but I know him, and keepe his saying.

text.NRSV: Your ancestor Abraham rejoiced that he would see my day; he saw it and was glad.”
text.Rotherham: Abraham, your father, exulted that he should see my day; and he saw, and rejoiced.
text.KJV: 8:56 Your father Abraham reioyced to see my day: and he saw it, & was glad.

text.NRSV: Then the Jews said to him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and have you seen Abraham?”
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, said unto him—Fifty years old, not yet art thou, and, Abraham, hast thou seen?
text.KJV: 8:57 Then said the Iewes vnto him, Thou art not yet fiftie yeeres olde, and hast thou seene Abraham?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, before Abraham was, I am.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—Verily, verily, I say unto you: Before, Abraham, came into existence, I, am.
text.KJV: 8:58 Iesus said vnto them, Uerely, verely I say vnto you, Before Abraham was, I am.

text.NRSV: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and went out of the temple.
text.Rotherham: They took up stones, therefore, that they might cast at him; but, Jesus, was hidden, and went forth out of the temple.
text.KJV: 8:59 Then tooke they vp stones to cast at him: but Iesus hidde himselfe, and went out of the Temple, going thorow the midst of them, and so passed by.

text.NRSV: As he walked along, he saw a man blind from birth.
text.Rotherham: And, passing along, he saw a man, blind from birth.
text.KJV: 9:1 And as Iesus passed by, he saw a man which was blinde from his birth.

text.NRSV: His disciples asked him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”
text.Rotherham: And his disciples questioned him, saying—Rabbi! who sinned, this man or his parents, that, blind, he should be born?
text.KJV: 9:2 And his disciples asked him, saying, Master, who did sinne, this man, or his parents, that he was borne blinde?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Neither this man nor his parents sinned; he was born blind so that God’s works might be revealed in him.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—Neither, this man, sinned nor his parents; but…that the works of God should be made manifest in him.
text.KJV: 9:3 Iesus answered, Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents: but that the workes of God should be made manifest in him.

text.NRSV: We must work the works of him who sent me while it is day; night is coming when no one can work.
text.Rotherham: We must needs be working the works of him that sent me, while it is, day: There cometh a night, when, no one, can work.
text.KJV: 9:4 I must worke the workes of him that sent me, while it is day: the night commeth when no man can worke.

text.NRSV: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”
text.Rotherham: Whensoever I may be, in the world, I am, the light, of the world.
text.KJV: 9:5 As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.

text.NRSV: When he had said this, he spat on the ground and made mud with the saliva and spread the mud on the man’s eyes,
text.Rotherham: These things, having said, he spat on the ground, and made clay with the spittle, and laid the clay upon his eyes;
text.KJV: 9:6 When he had thus spoken, he spat on the ground, and made clay of the spettle, and he anointed the eyes of the blinde man with the clay,

text.NRSV: saying to him, “Go, wash in the pool of Siloam” (which means Sent). Then he went and washed and came back able to see.
text.Rotherham: and said unto him—Withdraw! wash in the pool of Siloam,—which is to be translated, Sent. He went away, therefore, and washed, and came, seeing.
text.KJV: 9:7 And said vnto him, Goe wash in the poole of Siloam (which is by interpretation, Sent.) He went his way therfore, and washed, and came seeing.

text.NRSV: The neighbors and those who had seen him before as a beggar began to ask, “Is this not the man who used to sit and beg?”
text.Rotherham: The neighbours, therefore, and they who used to observe him aforetime—that he was, a beggar, were saying—Is not, this, he that used to sit and beg?
text.KJV: 9:8 ¶ The neighbours therefore, and they which before had seene him, that he was blinde, said, Is not this he that sate and begged?

text.NRSV: Some were saying, “It is he.” Others were saying, “No, but it is someone like him.” He kept saying, “I am the man.”
text.Rotherham: Others, were saying—’Tis, the same. Others, were saying—Nay! but he is, like him. He, was saying—I, am he.
text.KJV: 9:9 Some said, This is hee: others said, Hee is like him: but hee sayd, I am hee.

text.NRSV: But they kept asking him, “Then how were your eyes opened?”
text.Rotherham: So they were saying unto him—How [then] were thine eyes opened?
text.KJV: 9:10 Therefore said they vnto him, How were thine eyes opened?

text.NRSV: He answered, “The man called Jesus made mud, spread it on my eyes, and said to me, ‘Go to Siloam and wash.’ Then I went and washed and received my sight.”
text.Rotherham: He, answered—The man that is called Jesus, made, clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said unto me: Withdraw unto the pool of Siloam, and wash. Going away, therefore, and washing, I received sight.
text.KJV: 9:11 He answered and said, A man that is called Iesus, made clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said vnto me, Goe to the poole of Siloam, and wash: and I went and washed, and I receiued sight.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Where is he?” He said, “I do not know.”
text.Rotherham: And they said unto him—Where is, He? He saith—I know not.
text.KJV: 9:12 Then said they vnto him, Where is he? He said, I know not.

text.NRSV: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had formerly been blind.
text.Rotherham: They bring him unto the Pharisees—him at one time blind.
text.KJV: 9:13 ¶ They brought to the Pharisees him that aforetime was blind.

text.NRSV: Now it was a sabbath day when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.
text.Rotherham: Now it was Sabbath, on the day when Jesus made, the clay, and opened his eyes.
text.KJV: 9:14 And it was the Sabbath day when Iesus made the clay, and opened his eyes.

text.NRSV: Then the Pharisees also began to ask him how he had received his sight. He said to them, “He put mud on my eyes. Then I washed, and now I see.”
text.Rotherham: Again, therefore, the Pharisees also questioned him, as to how he received sight. And, he, said unto them—Clay, laid he upon mine eyes, and I washed,—and do see.
text.KJV: 9:15 Then againe the Pharisees also asked him how he had receiued his sight. He said vnto them, Hee put clay vpon mine eyes, and I washed, and doe see.

text.NRSV: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, for he does not observe the sabbath.” But others said, “How can a man who is a sinner perform such signs?” And they were divided.
text.Rotherham: Certain from among the Pharisees, therefore, were saying—This man is not, from God, because, the Sabbath, he keepeth not. Others, [however] were saying—How can a sinful man, such signs as these, be doing? And there was, a division, among them.
text.KJV: 9:16 Therefore said some of the Pharisees, This man is not of God, because hee keepeth not the Sabbath day. Others said, How can a man that is a sinner, doe such miracles? and there was a diuision among them.

text.NRSV: So they said again to the blind man, “What do you say about him? It was your eyes he opened.” He said, “He is a prophet.”
text.Rotherham: So they were saying unto the blind man, again, What dost, thou, say concerning him, in that he opened thine eyes? And, he, said—A prophet, is he.
text.KJV: 9:17 They say vnto the blind man againe, What sayest thou of him, that he hath opened thine eyes? He said, Hee is a Prophet.

text.NRSV: The Jews did not believe that he had been blind and had received his sight until they called the parents of the man who had received his sight
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, did not believe, concerning him, that he was blind, and received sight,—until they called the parents of him that had received sight,
text.KJV: 9:18 But the Iewes did not beleeue concerning him, that hee had bin blind, and receiued his sight, vntill they called the parents of him that had receiued his sight.

text.NRSV: and asked them, “Is this your son, who you say was born blind? How then does he now see?”
text.Rotherham: and questioned them, saying—Is, this, your son, of whom, ye, say, that blind, he was, born? How, then, seeth he, even now?
text.KJV: 9:19 And they asked them, saying, Is this your sonne, who ye say was borne blind? how then doth he now see?

text.NRSV: His parents answered, “We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;
text.Rotherham: His parents, therefore, answered, and said—We know that, this, is our son, and that, blind, he was born;
text.KJV: 9:20 His parents answered them, and said, We know that this is our sonne, and that he was borne blind:

text.NRSV: but we do not know how it is that now he sees, nor do we know who opened his eyes. Ask him; he is of age. He will speak for himself.”
text.Rotherham: But, how he now seeth, we know not, or, who opened his eyes, we, know not,—Question, him, he is, of age, he, concerning himself, shall speak.
text.KJV: 9:21 But by what meanes he now seeth, we know not, or who hath opened his eyes we know not: hee is of age, aske him, he shall speake for himselfe.

text.NRSV: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jews; for the Jews had already agreed that anyone who confessed Jesus to be the Messiah would be put out of the synagogue.
text.Rotherham: These things, said his parents, because they were in fear of the Jews,—for, already, had the Jews agreed together, that, if anyone should confess, him, to be Christ, an, excommunicant from the synagogue, should he be made.
text.KJV: 9:22 These words spake his parents, because they feared the Iewes: for the Iewes had agreed already, that if any man did confesse that he was Christ, he should be put out of the Synagogue.

text.NRSV: Therefore his parents said, “He is of age; ask him.”
text.Rotherham: For this cause, his parents said—He is, of age,—question him.
text.KJV: 9:23 Therefore said his parents, He is of age, aske him.

text.NRSV: So for the second time they called the man who had been blind, and they said to him, “Give glory to God! We know that this man is a sinner.”
text.Rotherham: So they called the man a second time—[him] who had been blind, and said unto him—Give glory unto God! We know that, this man, is, a sinner.
text.KJV: 9:24 Then againe called they the man that was blind, and said vnto him, Giue God the praise, we know that this man is a sinner.

text.NRSV: He answered, “I do not know whether he is a sinner. One thing I do know, that though I was blind, now I see.”
text.Rotherham: He, therefore, answered—Whether he is a sinner, I know not: One thing, I know,—That, whereas I was, blind, now, I see!
text.KJV: 9:25 He answered, and said, Whether he be a sinner or no, I know not: One thing I know, that whereas I was blind, now I see.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?”
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, unto him—What did he unto thee? How opened he thine eyes?
text.KJV: 9:26 Then saide they to him againe, What did he to thee? How opened hee thine eyes?

text.NRSV: He answered them, “I have told you already, and you would not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
text.Rotherham: He answered them—I told you just now, and ye did not hear: Why, again, do ye wish to hear? Are, ye also, wishing to become, his disciples?
text.KJV: 9:27 He answered them, I haue told you already, and ye did not heare: wherfore would you heare it againe? Will ye also be his disciples?

text.NRSV: Then they reviled him, saying, “You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.
text.Rotherham: And they reviled him, and said—Thou, art, the disciple, of that man; but, we, are, Moses’, disciples:—
text.KJV: 9:28 Then they reuiled him, and said, Thou art his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.

text.NRSV: We know that God has spoken to Moses, but as for this man, we do not know where he comes from.”
text.Rotherham: We, know, that, unto Moses, hath God spoken; but, as for this man, we know not whence he is.
text.KJV: 9:29 Wee know that God spake vnto Moses: as for this fellow, we knowe not from whence he is.

text.NRSV: The man answered, “Here is an astonishing thing! You do not know where he comes from, and yet he opened my eyes.
text.Rotherham: The man answered, and said unto them—Why! Herein, is, the marvel: That, ye, know not whence he is, and yet he opened mine eyes.
text.KJV: 9:30 The man answered, and said vnto them, Why herein is a marueilous thing, that ye know not from whence he is, and yet he hath opened mine eyes.

text.NRSV: We know that God does not listen to sinners, but he does listen to one who worships him and obeys his will.
text.Rotherham: We know that, God, unto sinners, doth not hearken: but, if one be, a worshipper of God, and be doing, his will, unto this one, he hearkeneth.
text.KJV: 9:31 Now we know that God heareth not sinners: but if any man bee a worshipper of God, and doth his will, him he heareth.

text.NRSV: Never since the world began has it been heard that anyone opened the eyes of a person born blind.
text.Rotherham: Out of age-past time, hath it never been heard, that anyone opened the eyes of one who, blind, had been born.
text.KJV: 9:32 Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was borne blinde:

text.NRSV: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing.”
text.Rotherham: If this man were not from God, he could have done nothing.
text.KJV: 9:33 If this man were not of God, he could doe nothing.

text.NRSV: They answered him, “You were born entirely in sins, and are you trying to teach us?” And they drove him out.
text.Rotherham: They answered and said unto him—In sins, wast, thou, born, altogether; and art, thou, teaching, us? And they cast him out.
text.KJV: 9:34 They answered, and saide vnto him, Thou wast altogether borne in sinnes, and doest thou teach vs? And they cast him out.

text.NRSV: Jesus heard that they had driven him out, and when he found him, he said, “Do you believe in the Son of Man?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus heard that they had cast him out: and, finding him, said—Dost, thou, believe on the son of Man?
text.KJV: 9:35 Iesus heard that they had cast him out; and when hee had found him, he said vnto him, Doest thou beleeue on the Sonne of God?

text.NRSV: He answered, “And who is he, sir? Tell me, so that I may believe in him.”
text.Rotherham: He answered [and said]—And, who, is he, Sir, that I may believe on him?
text.KJV: 9:36 He answered and said, Who is he, Lord, that I might beleeue on him?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “You have seen him, and the one speaking with you is he.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto him—Thou hast both seen him and, he that is speaking with thee, is, he.
text.KJV: 9:37 And Iesus said vnto him, Thou hast both seene him, and it is he that talketh with thee.

text.NRSV: He said, “Lord, I believe.” And he worshiped him.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—I believe, Sir! and worshipped him.
text.KJV: 9:38 And he said, Lord, I beleeue: and he worshipped him.

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “I came into this world for judgment so that those who do not see may see, and those who do see may become blind.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said—For judgment, I, unto this world, came: that, they who were not seeing, might see, and, they who were seeing, might become, blind.
text.KJV: 9:39 ¶ And Iesus said, For iudgment I am come into this world, that they which see not, might see, and that they which see, might be made blind.

text.NRSV: Some of the Pharisees near him heard this and said to him, “Surely we are not blind, are we?”
text.Rotherham: They of the Pharisees who were with him, heard, these things, and said unto him—Are, we also, blind?
text.KJV: 9:40 And some of the Pharisees which were with him, heard these words, and saide vnto him, Are wee blinde also?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “If you were blind, you would not have sin. But now that you say, ‘We see,’ your sin remains.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—If, blind, ye had been, ye had not had sin; but, now, ye say, We see, your sin, abideth.
text.KJV: 9:41 Iesus saide vnto them, If yee were blind, ye should haue no sinne: but now ye say, We see, therfore your sinne remaineth.

text.NRSV: “Very truly, I tell you, anyone who does not enter the sheepfold by the gate but climbs in by another way is a thief and a bandit.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you:—He that entereth not, through the door, into the fold of the sheep, but goeth up from another place, that man, is, a thief, and, a robber.
text.KJV: 10:1 Uerily, verily I say vnto you, He that entreth not by þ doore into the sheepefold, but climeth vp some other way, the same is a theefe, and a robber.

text.NRSV: The one who enters by the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
text.Rotherham: But, he that entereth through the door, is, shepherd, of the sheep:
text.KJV: 10:2 But hee that entreth in by the doore, is the shepherd of the sheepe.

text.NRSV: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep hear his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
text.Rotherham: To him, the porter openeth, and, the sheep, unto his voice, hearken: and, his own sheep, he calleth by name, and leadeth them forth.
text.KJV: 10:3 To him the porter openeth, and the sheepe heare his voyce, and he calleth his owne sheepe by name, and leadeth them out.

text.NRSV: When he has brought out all his own, he goes ahead of them, and the sheep follow him because they know his voice.
text.Rotherham: As soon as, all his own, he hath put forth, before them, he moveth on, and, the sheep, follow him, because they know his voice;
text.KJV: 10:4 And when he putteth foorth his owne sheepe, he goeth before them, and the sheepe follow him: for they know his voyce.

text.NRSV: They will not follow a stranger, but they will run from him because they do not know the voice of strangers.”
text.Rotherham: But, a stranger, will they in nowise follow, but will flee from him, because they know not the voice, of strangers.
text.KJV: 10:5 And a stranger will they not follow, but will flee from him, for they know not the voyce of strangers.

text.NRSV: Jesus used this figure of speech with them, but they did not understand what he was saying to them.
text.Rotherham: This similitude, spake Jesus unto them; but, those men, understood not what the things were which he was speaking unto them.
text.KJV: 10:6 This parable spake Iesus vnto them: but they vnderstood not what things they were which he spake vnto them.

text.NRSV: So again Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, I am the gate for the sheep.
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said, again—Verily, verily, I say unto you:—I, am the door of the sheep:
text.KJV: 10:7 Then said Iesus vnto them againe, Uerily, verily I say vnto you, I am the doore of the sheepe.

text.NRSV: All who came before me are thieves and bandits; but the sheep did not listen to them.
text.Rotherham: All, as many as came before me, are thieves, and, robbers: but the sheep hearkened nor unto them.
text.KJV: 10:8 All that euer came before me, are theeues and robbers: but the sheepe did not heare them.

text.NRSV: I am the gate. Whoever enters by me will be saved, and will come in and go out and find pasture.
text.Rotherham: I, am the door: through me, if anyone enter, he shall be saved, and shall come in and go out, and, pasture, shall find.
text.KJV: 10:9 I am the doore by me if any man enter in, he shall be saued, and shall goe in and out, and find pasture.

text.NRSV: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I came that they may have life, and have it abundantly.
text.Rotherham: The thief, cometh not, save that he may thieve and slay and destroy: I, came, that, life, they might have, and, above measure, might have.
text.KJV: 10:10 The theefe commeth not, but for to steale and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might haue life, and that they might haue it more abundantly.

text.NRSV: “I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
text.Rotherham: I, am the good shepherd: The good shepherd, his life, layeth down, for the sheep.
text.KJV: 10:11 I am the good shepheard: the good shepheard giueth his life for the sheepe.

text.NRSV: The hired hand, who is not the shepherd and does not own the sheep, sees the wolf coming and leaves the sheep and runs away—and the wolf snatches them and scatters them.
