text.KJV: 15:6 If a man abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered, and men gather them, and cast them into the fire, and they are burned.

text.NRSV: If you abide in me, and my words abide in you, ask for whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.
text.Rotherham: If ye abide in me, and, my sayings, in you, abide, whatsoever ye may be desiring, ask! and it shall be brought to pass for you.
text.KJV: 15:7 If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall aske what ye will, and it shall be done vnto you.

text.NRSV: My Father is glorified by this, that you bear much fruit and become my disciples.
text.Rotherham: Herein, was my Father glorified, that, much fruit, ye should bear, and become my disciples.
text.KJV: 15:8 Herein is my Father glorified, that ye beare much fruit, so shall ye bee my Disciples.

text.NRSV: As the Father has loved me, so I have loved you; abide in my love.
text.Rotherham: Just as the Father loved me, I also, loved you: Abide ye in my love.
text.KJV: 15:9 As the Father hath loued me, so haue I loued you: continue ye in my loue.

text.NRSV: If you keep my commandments, you will abide in my love, just as I have kept my Father’s commandments and abide in his love.
text.Rotherham: If, my commandments, ye keep, ye shall abide in my love,—just as, I, the Father’s commandments, have kept, and abide in his love.
text.KJV: 15:10 If ye keepe my Commandements, ye shal abide in my loue, euen as I haue kept my Fathers Commandements, and abide in his loue.

text.NRSV: I have said these things to you so that my joy may be in you, and that your joy may be complete.
text.Rotherham: These things, have I spoken unto you, that, my own joy, in you, may be, and, your joy, may be made full.
text.KJV: 15:11 These things haue I spoken vnto you, that my ioy might remaine in you, and that your ioy might be full.

text.NRSV: “This is my commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you.
text.Rotherham: This, is my own commandment, That ye be loving one another, just as I loved you.
text.KJV: 15:12 This is my Commaundement, that ye loue one another, as I haue loued you.

text.NRSV: No one has greater love than this, to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.
text.Rotherham: Greater love than this, hath, no one, That, his life, one should lay down in behalf of his friends.
text.KJV: 15:13 Greater loue hath no man then this, that a man lay downe his life for his friends.

text.NRSV: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are, friends of mine, if ye be doing that which, I, am commanding you.
text.KJV: 15:14 Ye are my friends, if ye do whatsoeuer I command you.

text.NRSV: I do not call you servants any longer, because the servant does not know what the master is doing; but I have called you friends, because I have made known to you everything that I have heard from my Father.
text.Rotherham: No longer, do I call you, servants, for, the servant, knoweth not what, his lord, is doing. But, you, I have called, friends, because, all things which I heard from my Father, made I known unto you.
text.KJV: 15:15 Henceforth I call you not seruants, for the seruant knoweth not what his lord doth, but I haue called you friends: for all things that I haue heard of my Father, I haue made knowen vnto you.

text.NRSV: You did not choose me but I chose you. And I appointed you to go and bear fruit, fruit that will last, so that the Father will give you whatever you ask him in my name.
text.Rotherham: Not, ye, chose, me, but, I, chose you, and placed you, that ye should go your way and bear, fruit,—and, your fruit, should abide: that, whatsoever ye should ask the Father in my name, he might give unto you.
text.KJV: 15:16 Ye haue not chosen me, but I haue chosen you, and ordeined you, that you should goe and bring foorth fruit, and that your fruite should remaine: that whatsoeuer ye shall aske of the Father in my Name, he may giue it you.

text.NRSV: I am giving you these commands so that you may love one another.
text.Rotherham: These things, I command you, That ye be loving one another.
text.KJV: 15:17 These things I commaund you, that ye loue one another.

text.NRSV: “If the world hates you, be aware that it hated me before it hated you.
text.Rotherham: If, the world, is hating, you, ye are getting to know that, me, before you, it hath hated.
text.KJV: 15:18 If the world hate you, yee know that it hated me before it hated you.

text.NRSV: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. Because you do not belong to the world, but I have chosen you out of the world—therefore the world hates you.
text.Rotherham: If, of the world, ye had been, the world, of its own, had been fond; but, because, of the world, ye are not, on the contrary, I, chose you out of the world, therefore, the world, doth hate you.
text.KJV: 15:19 If ye were of the world, the world would loue his owne: But because yee are not of the world, but I haue chosen you out of the world, therfore the world hateth you.

text.NRSV: Remember the word that I said to you, ‘Servants are not greater than their master.’ If they persecuted me, they will persecute you; if they kept my word, they will keep yours also.
text.Rotherham: Remember the word which, I, spake unto you: A servant is not greater than his lord. If, me, they persecuted, you too, will they persecute,—If, my word, they kept, your own also, will they keep.
text.KJV: 15:20 Remember the word that I said vnto you, The seruant is not greater then the Lord: if they haue persecuted me, they will also persecute you: if they haue kept my saying, they will keepe yours also.

text.NRSV: But they will do all these things to you on account of my name, because they do not know the one who sent me.
text.Rotherham: But, all these things, will they do unto you, on account of my name, because they know not him that sent me.
text.KJV: 15:21 But all these things will they doe vnto you for my Names sake, because they know not him that sent me.

text.NRSV: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin.
text.Rotherham: Had I not come and spoken unto them, Sin, had they none; but, now, have they no, excuse, for their sin.
text.KJV: 15:22 If I had not come, and spoken vnto them, they had not had sinne: but now they haue no cloke for their sinne.

text.NRSV: Whoever hates me hates my Father also.
text.Rotherham: He that hateth me, hateth, my Father also.
text.KJV: 15:23 He that hateth me, hateth my Father also.

text.NRSV: If I had not done among them the works that no one else did, they would not have sin; but now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
text.Rotherham: Had I not done among them, the works, which, no other, had done, sin, had they none; but, now, have they, both seen and hated both me and my Father.
text.KJV: 15:24 If I had not done among thē the works which none other man did, they had not had sinne: but now haue they both seene, & hated both me & my father.

text.NRSV: It was to fulfill the word that is written in their law, ‘They hated me without a cause.’
text.Rotherham: But…that the word which, in their law, is written, might be fulfilled—They hated me without cause.
text.KJV: 15:25 But this commeth to passe, that the word might be fulfilled that is written in their law, They hated me without a cause.

text.NRSV: “When the Advocate comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth who comes from the Father, he will testify on my behalf.
text.Rotherham: Whensoever the Advocate shall come, Whom, I, will send unto you from the Father, The Spirit of truth, which, from the Father, cometh forth, He, will bear witness concerning me;
text.KJV: 15:26 But when the Comforter is come, whom I wil send vnto you from the Father, euen the Spirit of trueth, which proceedeth from the Father, hee shall testifie of me.

text.NRSV: You also are to testify because you have been with me from the beginning.”
text.Rotherham: And do, ye also, bear witness, because, from the beginning, ye are, with me,
text.KJV: 15:27 And ye also shall beare witnesse, because ye haue bene with me from the beginning.

text.NRSV: “I have said these things to you to keep you from stumbling.
text.Rotherham: These things, have I spoken unto you, that ye may not be caused to stumble:
text.KJV: 16:1 These things haue I spoken vnto you, that yee should not be offended.

text.NRSV: They will put you out of the synagogues. Indeed, an hour is coming when those who kill you will think that by doing so they are offering worship to God.
text.Rotherham: Excommunicants from the synagogue, will they make you; Nay! there cometh an hour, that, every one who killeth you, shall think to be rendering, divine service, unto God!
text.KJV: 16:2 They shall put you out of the Synagogues: yea, the time commeth, that whosoeuer killeth you, will thinke that hee doeth God seruice.

text.NRSV: And they will do this because they have not known the Father or me.
text.Rotherham: And, these things, will they do, because they got to know, neither the Father nor me.
text.KJV: 16:3 And these things will they doe vnto you, because they haue not knowen the Father, nor me.

text.NRSV: But I have said these things to you so that when their hour comes you may remember that I told you of them. “I did not say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
text.Rotherham: But, these things, have I told you,—That, whensoever their hour shall come, ye may remember, that, thereof, I told you. These things, however, I told you not, from the beginning, because I was, with you;
text.KJV: 16:4 But these things haue I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not vnto you at the beginning, because I was with you.

text.NRSV: But now I am going to him who sent me; yet none of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’
text.Rotherham: But, now, I go my way unto him that sent me, and, not one from among you, questioneth me—Whither goest thou?
text.KJV: 16:5 But now I goe my way to him that sent mee, and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou?

text.NRSV: But because I have said these things to you, sorrow has filled your hearts.
text.Rotherham: But, because, these things, I have told you, sorrow, hath filled your heart.
text.KJV: 16:6 But because I haue saide these things vnto you, sorow hath filled your heart.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless I tell you the truth: it is to your advantage that I go away, for if I do not go away, the Advocate will not come to you; but if I go, I will send him to you.
text.Rotherham: But, I, the truth, am telling you—It is profitable for you, that, I, depart; for, if I should not depart, The Advocate, would in nowise come unto you, but, if I go, I will send him unto you.
text.KJV: 16:7 Neuerthelesse; I tell you the trueth, it is expedient for you that I goe away: for if I goe not away, the Comforter will not come vnto you: but if I depart, I will send him vnto you.

text.NRSV: And when he comes, he will prove the world wrong about sin and righteousness and judgment:
text.Rotherham: And, having come, He, will reprove the world—Concerning sin, and concerning righteousness, and concerning judgment:
text.KJV: 16:8 And when he is come, he will reproue the world of sinne, and of righteousnesse, and of iudgement.

text.NRSV: about sin, because they do not believe in me;
text.Rotherham: Concerning sin, indeed, because they are not believing on me;
text.KJV: 16:9 Of sinne, because they beleeue not on me.

text.NRSV: about righteousness, because I am going to the Father and you will see me no longer;
text.Rotherham: But, concerning righteousness, because, unto the Father, I go my way, and, no longer, do ye behold me;
text.KJV: 16:10 Of righteousnesse, because I goe to my Father, and ye see me no more.

text.NRSV: about judgment, because the ruler of this world has been condemned.
text.Rotherham: And, concerning judgment, because, the ruler of this world, hath been judged.
text.KJV: 16:11 Of iudgement, because the prince of this world is iudged.

text.NRSV: “I still have many things to say to you, but you cannot bear them now.
text.Rotherham: Yet many things, have I, unto you, to be saying,—but ye cannot bear them, just now;
text.KJV: 16:12 I haue yet many things to say vnto you, but ye cannot beare them now:

text.NRSV: When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all the truth; for he will not speak on his own, but will speak whatever he hears, and he will declare to you the things that are to come.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, as soon as, he, hath come—The Spirit of truth, he will guide you into all truth; for he will not speak from himself, but, whatsoever he heareth, he will speak, and, the coming things, will he announce unto you.
text.KJV: 16:13 Howbeit, when hee the spirit of trueth is come, he wil guide you into all trueth: For he shall not speake of himselfe: but whatsoeuer he shall heare, that shall he speake, and he will shew you things to come.

text.NRSV: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and declare it to you.
text.Rotherham: He, shall glorify me; for, of mine, shall he receive, and announce unto you.
text.KJV: 16:14 He shall glorifie me, for he shall receiue of mine, and shall shew it vnto you.

text.NRSV: All that the Father has is mine. For this reason I said that he will take what is mine and declare it to you.
text.Rotherham: All things, whatsoever the Father hath, are, my own; therefore, said I—Of mine, shall he receive, and announce unto you.
text.KJV: 16:15 All things that the Father hath, are mine: therefore said I that he shall take of mine, and shal shew it vnto you.

text.NRSV: “A little while, and you will no longer see me, and again a little while, and you will see me.”
text.Rotherham: A little while, and, no longer, ye behold me; and, again a little while, and ye shall see me.
text.KJV: 16:16 A litle while, and ye shall not see me: and againe a litle while, & ye shall see me: because I goe to the Father.

text.NRSV: Then some of his disciples said to one another, “What does he mean by saying to us, ‘A little while, and you will no longer see me, and again a little while, and you will see me’; and ‘Because I am going to the Father’?”
text.Rotherham: Some of his disciples, therefore, said one to another—What is this which he is saying to us:—A little while, and ye behold me not, and, again a little while, and ye shall see me; and—Because I go my way unto the Father?
text.KJV: 16:17 Then saide some of his disciples among themselues, What is this that he saith vnto vs, A litle while, and ye shal not see me: and againe, a litle while, and ye shall see me: and, because I goe to the Father?

text.NRSV: They said, “What does he mean by this ‘a little while’? We do not know what he is talking about.”
text.Rotherham: They were saying, therefore—What is this which he saith:—A little while? We know not [what he is saying].
text.KJV: 16:18 They said therefore, What is this that he saith, A litle while? we cannot tell what he saith.

text.NRSV: Jesus knew that they wanted to ask him, so he said to them, “Are you discussing among yourselves what I meant when I said, ‘A little while, and you will no longer see me, and again a little while, and you will see me’?
text.Rotherham: Jesus took note, that they were wishing to question him, and said unto them—Concerning this, are ye enquiring one with another,—because I said:—A little while, and ye behold me not, and, again, a little while, and ye shall see me?
text.KJV: 16:19 Now Iesus knew that they were desirous to aske him, & said vnto them, Doe ye enquire among your selues of that I saide, A litle while, and ye shall not see mee: and againe; A little while and ye shall see me?

text.NRSV: Very truly, I tell you, you will weep and mourn, but the world will rejoice; you will have pain, but your pain will turn into joy.
text.Rotherham: Verily, verily, I say unto you—Ye, shall weep and lament, but, the world, shall rejoice: Ye, shall be grieved, but, your grief, into joy, shall be turned.
text.KJV: 16:20 Uerily, verily I say vnto you, that ye shall weepe and lament, but the world shall reioyce: And ye shall be sorrowfull, but your sorrow shall be turned into ioy.

text.NRSV: When a woman is in labor, she has pain, because her hour has come. But when her child is born, she no longer remembers the anguish because of the joy of having brought a human being into the world.
text.Rotherham: A woman, as soon as she is about to bring forth, hath, grief, because her hour hath come; but, as soon as she hath given birth to the child, no longer, remembereth she the anguish, by reason of the joy, that a human being into the world hath been born.
text.KJV: 16:21 A woman, when she is in trauaile, hath sorrow, because her houre is come: but assoone as she is deliuered of the child, she remembreth no more the anguish, for ioy that a man is borne into the world.

text.NRSV: So you have pain now; but I will see you again, and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take your joy from you.
text.Rotherham: And, ye, therefore, now, indeed have grief; but, again, will I see you, and your heart shall rejoice,—and, your joy, no one, shall force from you.
text.KJV: 16:22 And ye now therefore haue sorrow: but I will see you againe, and your heart shall reioyce, and your ioy no man taketh from you.

text.NRSV: On that day you will ask nothing of me. Very truly, I tell you, if you ask anything of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.
text.Rotherham: And, in that day, shall ye request me, nothing:—Verily, verily, I say unto you—Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father, He will give you, in my name.
text.KJV: 16:23 And in that day ye shall aske me nothing: Uerily, verily I say vnto you, Whatsoeuer yee shall aske the Father in my Name, he will giue it you.

text.NRSV: Until now you have not asked for anything in my name. Ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be complete.
text.Rotherham: Until even now, ye have asked nothing in my name: Be asking, and ye shall receive,—that, your joy, may be made full.
text.KJV: 16:24 Hitherto haue ye asked nothing in my Name: aske, and ye shall receiue, that your ioy may be full.

text.NRSV: “I have said these things to you in figures of speech. The hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures, but will tell you plainly of the Father.
text.Rotherham: These things, in similitudes, have I spoken unto you: There cometh an hour, when, no longer in similitudes, will I speak unto you, but, openly, concerning the Father, will I tell you.
text.KJV: 16:25 These things haue I spoken vnto you in prouerbs: the time commeth when I shall no more speake vnto you in prouerbes, but I shall shew you plainly of the Father.

text.NRSV: On that day you will ask in my name. I do not say to you that I will ask the Father on your behalf;
text.Rotherham: In that day, In my name, shall ye ask:—and I say not that, I, will request the Father for you;
text.KJV: 16:26 At that day ye shall aske in my Name: and I say not vnto you that I will pray the Father for you:

text.NRSV: for the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have believed that I came from God.
text.Rotherham: For, the Father himself, dearly loveth you, because, ye, have dearly loved me, and believed that, I, from the Father, came forth:—
text.KJV: 16:27 For the Father himselfe loueth you, because ye haue loued me and haue beleeued that I came out from God.

text.NRSV: I came from the Father and have come into the world; again, I am leaving the world and am going to the Father.”
text.Rotherham: I came forth out of the Father, and have come into the world,—Again, I leave the world, and go, unto the Father.
text.KJV: 16:28 I came foorth from the Father, and am come into the world: againe, I leaue the world, and goe to the Father.

text.NRSV: His disciples said, “Yes, now you are speaking plainly, not in figures of speech!
text.Rotherham: His disciples say—See! now, openly, art thou speaking, and, not a single similitude, art thou using:
text.KJV: 16:29 His disciples said vnto him, Loe, now speakest thou plainly, and speakest no prouerbe.

text.NRSV: Now we know that you know all things, and do not need to have anyone question you; by this we believe that you came from God.”
text.Rotherham: Now, we know, that thou knowest all things, and hast, no need, that one be questioning thee. Hereby, do we believe, that, from God, thou camest forth.
text.KJV: 16:30 Now are we sure that thou knowest al things, and needest not that any man should aske thee: By this we beleeue that thou camest foorth from God.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Do you now believe?
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered them—As yet, ye believe:
text.KJV: 16:31 Iesus answered them, Doe yee now beleeue?

text.NRSV: The hour is coming, indeed it has come, when you will be scattered, each one to his home, and you will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone because the Father is with me.
text.Rotherham: Lo! there cometh an hour, and hath come, that ye should be scattered, each, unto his own home; and, me, alone, should leave;—And yet I am not, alone, but, the Father, is, with me!
text.KJV: 16:32 Behold, the houre commeth, yea is now come, that ye shall be scattered, euery man to his owne, and shall leaue me alone: and yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.

text.NRSV: I have said this to you, so that in me you may have peace. In the world you have tribulation. But take courage; I have conquered the world!”
text.Rotherham: These things, have I spoken unto you, that, in me, ye may have, peace: In the world, ye have, tribulation; but be taking courage,—I, have overcome the world.
text.KJV: 16:33 These things I haue spoken vnto you, that in me ye might haue peace, in the world ye shall haue tribulation: but be of good cheare, I haue ouercome the world.

text.NRSV: After Jesus had spoken these words, he looked up to heaven and said, “Father, the hour has come; glorify your Son so that the Son may glorify you,
text.Rotherham: These things, spake Jesus, and, lifting up his eyes unto heaven, said:—Father! The hour is come! Glorify thy Son, that, the Son, may glorify, thee,—
text.KJV: 17:1 These words spake Iesus, and lift vp his eyes to heauen, and said, Father, the houre is come, glorifie thy Sonne, that thy Sonne also may glorifie thee.

text.NRSV: since you have given him authority over all people, to give eternal life to all whom you have given him.
text.Rotherham: Even as thou gavest him authority over all flesh, that, as touching whatsoever thou hast given him, he might give unto them, life age-abiding.
text.KJV: 17:2 As thou hast giuen him power ouer all flesh, that he should giue eternall life to as many as thou hast giuen him.

text.NRSV: And this is eternal life, that they may know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the age-abiding life, That they get to know thee, the only real God, and him whom thou didst send, Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 17:3 And this is life eternall, that they might know thee the onely true God, and Iesus Christ whom thou hast sent.

text.NRSV: I glorified you on earth by finishing the work that you gave me to do.
text.Rotherham: I, glorified thee on the earth, The work, finishing, which thou hast given me that I should do.
text.KJV: 17:4 I haue glorified thee on the earth: I haue finished the worke which thou gauest me to doe.

text.NRSV: So now, Father, glorify me in your own presence with the glory that I had in your presence before the world existed.
text.Rotherham: And, now, glorify me—thou, Father! with thyself, with the glory which I had, before the world’s existence, with thee.
text.KJV: 17:5 And now O Father, glorifie thou me, with thine owne selfe, with the glory which I had with thee before the world was.

text.NRSV: “I have made your name known to those whom you gave me from the world. They were yours, and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
text.Rotherham: I manifested thy name, unto the men whom thou gavest me out of the world: Thine, they were, and, to me, thou gavest them, and, thy word, have they kept:
text.KJV: 17:6 I haue manifested thy Name vnto the men which thou gauest me out of the world: thine they were; and thou gauest them me; and they haue kept thy word.

text.NRSV: Now they know that everything you have given me is from you;
text.Rotherham: Now, have they come to know, That, all things, as many as thou gavest me, are, from thee;
text.KJV: 17:7 Now they haue knowen that all things whatsoeuer thou hast giuen me, are of thee.

text.NRSV: for the words that you gave to me I have given to them, and they have received them and know in truth that I came from you; and they have believed that you sent me.
text.Rotherham: And, the declarations which thou gavest me, have I given them, and, they, received them, and came to know in truth, that, from thee, came I forth,—and they believed, that, thou, didst send me forth.
text.KJV: 17:8 For I haue giuen vnto them the words which thou gauest me, and they haue receiued them, and haue knowen surely that I came out from thee, and they haue beleeued that thou didst send me.

text.NRSV: I am asking on their behalf; I am not asking on behalf of the world, but on behalf of those whom you gave me, because they are yours.
text.Rotherham: I, concerning them, make request: not, concerning the world, do I make request, but concerning them whom thou hast given me, because, thine, they are,—
text.KJV: 17:9 I pray for them, I pray not for the world: but for them which thou hast giuen me, for they are thine.

text.NRSV: All mine are yours, and yours are mine; and I have been glorified in them.
text.Rotherham: And, all my possessions, are, thine, and, thy possessions, mine, and I have been glorified in them.
text.KJV: 17:10 And all mine are thine, and thine are mine: and I am glorified in them.

text.NRSV: And now I am no longer in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, protect them in your name that you have given me, so that they may be one, as we are one.
text.Rotherham: And, no longer, am I in the world, and, they, are, in the world,—and, I, unto thee, am coming. Holy Father! Keep them in thy name which thou hast given me, that they may be one, as, we.
text.KJV: 17:11 And now I am no more in the world, but these are in the world, and I come to thee. Holy Father, keep through thine owne Name, those whom thou hast giuen mee, that they may bee one, as we are.

text.NRSV: While I was with them, I protected them in your name that you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost except the one destined to be lost, so that the scripture might be fulfilled.
text.Rotherham: When I was with them, I, kept them in thy name which thou hast given me,—And I kept watch, and, none from among them, went the destruction,—save the son of destruction, that, the Scripture, might be fulfilled.
text.KJV: 17:12 While I was with them in the world, I kept them in thy Name: those that thou gauest me, I haue kept, and none of them is lost, but the sonne of perdition: that the Scripture might be fulfilled.

text.NRSV: But now I am coming to you, and I speak these things in the world so that they may have my joy made complete in themselves.
text.Rotherham: But, now, unto thee, am I coming; and, these things, am I speaking in the world, that they may have my own joy fulfilled in themselves.
text.KJV: 17:13 And now come I to thee, and these things I speake in the world, that they might haue my ioy fulfilled in themselues.

text.NRSV: I have given them your word, and the world has hated them because they do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.
text.Rotherham: I, have given them thy word, and, the world, hated them, because they are not of the world—even as, I, am not of the world.
text.KJV: 17:14 I haue giuen them thy word, and the world hath hated them, because they are not of the world, euen as I am not of the world.

text.NRSV: I am not asking you to take them out of the world, but I ask you to protect them from the evil one.
text.Rotherham: I request not, that thou wouldst take them out of the world, but that thou wouldst keep them out of the evil:
text.KJV: 17:15 I pray not that thou shouldest take them out of the world, but that thou shouldest keepe them from the euill.

text.NRSV: They do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.
text.Rotherham: Of the world, they are not, even as, I, am not of the world.
text.KJV: 17:16 They are not of the world, euen as I am not of the world.

text.NRSV: Sanctify them in the truth; your word is truth.
text.Rotherham: Hallow them in the truth: Thine own word, is, truth:
text.KJV: 17:17 Sanctifie them through thy trueth: thy word is trueth.

text.NRSV: As you have sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
text.Rotherham: Even as thou didst send me forth into the world, I also, send them forth into the world;
text.KJV: 17:18 As thou hast sent mee into the world: euen so haue I also sent them into the world.

text.NRSV: And for their sakes I sanctify myself, so that they also may be sanctified in truth.
text.Rotherham: And, on their behalf, I, hallow myself, that, they also, may have become hallowed in truth.
text.KJV: 17:19 And for their sakes I sanctifie my selfe, that they also might be sanctified through the trueth.

text.NRSV: “I ask not only on behalf of these, but also on behalf of those who will believe in me through their word,
text.Rotherham: Not however concerning these alone, do I make request, but, concerning them also who believe, through their word, on me:
text.KJV: 17:20 Neither pray I for these alone; but for them also which shall beleeue on me through their word:

text.NRSV: that they may all be one. As you, Father, are in me and I am in you, may they also be in us, so that the world may believe that you have sent me.
text.Rotherham: That they, all, may be, one,—even as, thou, Father, in me, and, I, in thee,—that, they also, in us, may be;—that, the world, may believe that, thou, didst send me forth.
text.KJV: 17:21 That they all may be one, as thou Father art in mee, and I in thee, that they also may bee one in vs: that the world may beleeue that thou hast sent mee.

text.NRSV: The glory that you have given me I have given them, so that they may be one, as we are one,
text.Rotherham: And, I, the glory which thou hast given to me, have given to them, that they may be one, even as, we, are, one.—
text.KJV: 17:22 And the glory which thou gauest me, I haue giuen them: that they may be one, euen as we are one:

text.NRSV: I in them and you in me, that they may become completely one, so that the world may know that you have sent me and have loved them even as you have loved me.
text.Rotherham: I, in them and, thou, in me; That they may have been perfected into, one,—That the world may get to know, that, thou, didst send me forth, and didst love them even as thou didst love, me.
text.KJV: 17:23 I in them, and thou in mee, that they may bee made perfect in one, and that the world may know that thou hast sent me, and hast loued them, as thou hast loued me.

text.NRSV: Father, I desire that those also, whom you have given me, may be with me where I am, to see my glory, which you have given me because you loved me before the foundation of the world.
text.Rotherham: Father! As touching that which thou hast given me, I desire—That, where, I, am, they also, may be, with me, that they may behold my own glory which thou hast given me,—because thou lovedst me, before the foundation of the world.
text.KJV: 17:24 Father, I will that they also whom thou hast giuen me, be with me where I am, that they may behold my glory which thou hast giuen mee: for thou louedst mee before the foundation of the world.

text.NRSV: “Righteous Father, the world does not know you, but I know you; and these know that you have sent me.
text.Rotherham: Righteous Father! And so, the world, came not to know thee; but, I, came to know thee, and, these, came to know that, thou, didst send me forth:
text.KJV: 17:25 O righteous Father, the world hath not knowen thee, but I haue knowen thee, and these haue knowen that thou hast sent me.

text.NRSV: I made your name known to them, and I will make it known, so that the love with which you have loved me may be in them, and I in them.”
text.Rotherham: And I made known unto them thy name, and will make known,—that, the love wherewith thou lovedst me, in them, may be, and, I, in them.
text.KJV: 17:26 And I haue declared vnto them thy Name, and will declare it: that the loue wherewith thou hast loued mee, may be in them, and I in them.

text.NRSV: After Jesus had spoken these words, he went out with his disciples across the Kidron valley to a place where there was a garden, which he and his disciples entered.
text.Rotherham: Having said these things, Jesus, went out, with his disciples, across the winter-torrent of the Kedron, where was a garden,—into which he entered, he, and his disciples.
text.KJV: 18:1 When Iesus had spoken these wordes, hee went foorth with his disciples ouer the Brooke Cedron, where was a garden, into the which hee entred and his disciples.

text.NRSV: Now Judas, who betrayed him, also knew the place, because Jesus often met there with his disciples.
text.Rotherham: Now, Judas also, who was delivering him up, knew the place: because, oft, had Jesus been gathered there, with his disciples.
text.KJV: 18:2 And Iudas also which betrayed him, knew the place: for Iesus oft times resorted thither with his disciples.

text.NRSV: So Judas brought a detachment of soldiers together with police from the chief priests and the Pharisees, and they came there with lanterns and torches and weapons.
text.Rotherham: Judas, therefore, receiving the band, and officers, from among the High-priests and [from among] the Pharisees, cometh thither, with lights and torches and weapons.
text.KJV: 18:3 Iudas then hauing receiued a band of men, and officers from the chiefe Priests and Pharisees, commeth thither with lanternes and torches, and weapons.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus, knowing all that was to happen to him, came forward and asked them, “Whom are you looking for?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, knowing all the things coming upon him, went forth, and saith unto them—Whom, seek ye?
text.KJV: 18:4 Iesus therefore knowing all things that should come vpon him, went foorth, and sayde vnto them, Whom seeke ye?

text.NRSV: They answered, “Jesus of Nazareth.” Jesus replied, “I am he.” Judas, who betrayed him, was standing with them.
text.Rotherham: They answered him—Jesus, the Nazarene. He saith unto them—I, am he. Now Judas also, who was delivering him up, was standing with them.
text.KJV: 18:5 They answered him, Iesus of Nazareth. Iesus saith vnto them, I am hee. And Iudas also which betraied him, stood with them.

text.NRSV: When Jesus said to them, “I am he,” they stepped back and fell to the ground.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, he said unto them, I, am he, they went backwards, and fell to the ground.
text.KJV: 18:6 Assoone then as he had said vnto them, I am he, they went backeward, and fell to the ground.

text.NRSV: Again he asked them, “Whom are you looking for?” And they said, “Jesus of Nazareth.”
text.Rotherham: Again, therefore, he questioned them—Whom, seek ye? And, they, said—Jesus, the Nazarene.
text.KJV: 18:7 Then asked hee them againe, Whom seeke ye? And they said, Iesus of Nazareth.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “I told you that I am he. So if you are looking for me, let these men go.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—I told you, I, am he. If, then, ye seek me, let these go their way:—
text.KJV: 18:8 Iesus answered, I haue tolde you that I am he: If therefore ye seeke me, let these goe their way:

text.NRSV: This was to fulfill the word that he had spoken, “I did not lose a single one of those whom you gave me.”
text.Rotherham: that the word might be fulfilled which he had said—As touching them whom thou hast given me, I lost from among them, not so much as one.
text.KJV: 18:9 That the saying might be fulfilled which he spake, Of them which thou gauest me, haue I lost none.

text.NRSV: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it, struck the high priest’s slave, and cut off his right ear. The slave’s name was Malchus.
text.Rotherham: Simon Peter, therefore, having a sword, drew it, and smote the High-priest’s servant, and cut off his right ear. Now the name of the servant was Malchus.
text.KJV: 18:10 Then Simon Peter hauing a sword, drewe it, and smote the high Priests seruant, & cut off his right eare: The seruants name was Malchus.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to Peter, “Put your sword back into its sheath. Am I not to drink the cup that the Father has given me?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said unto Peter—Thrust the sword into its sheath:—The cup which the Father hath given me, shall I in anywise not drink it?
text.KJV: 18:11 Then said Iesus vnto Peter, Put vp thy sword into the sheath: the cup which my father hath giuen me, shall I not drinke it?

text.NRSV: So the detachment of soldiers with their officer and the Jewish police arrested Jesus and bound him.
text.Rotherham: The band, therefore, and the captain, and the officers of the Jews, apprehended Jesus, and bound him,
text.KJV: 18:12 Then the band and the captaine, and officers of the Iewes, tooke Iesus, and bound him,

text.NRSV: First they took him to Annas, who was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year.
text.Rotherham: and led him unto Annas, first; for he was father-in-law of Caiaphas, who was the High-priest of that year.
text.KJV: 18:13 And led him away to Annas first, (for he was father in law to Caiaphas) which was the high Priest that same yeere.

text.NRSV: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jews that it was better to have one person die for the people.
text.Rotherham: Now Caiaphas was he that gave counsel unto the Jews, that it was profitable for, one man, to die in behalf of the people.
text.KJV: 18:14 Now Caiaphas was he which gaue counsell to the Iewes, that it was expedient that one man should die for the people.

text.NRSV: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Since that disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest,
text.Rotherham: And Simon Peter was following with Jesus, also another disciple. But, that disciple, was known unto the High-priest, and entered in, with Jesus, into the court of the High-priest;
text.KJV: 18:15 ¶ And Simon Peter followed Iesus, and so did another disciple: that disciple was knowen vnto the high Priest, and went in with Iesus into the palace of the high Priest.

text.NRSV: but Peter was standing outside at the gate. So the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out, spoke to the woman who guarded the gate, and brought Peter in.
text.Rotherham: whereas, Peter, remained standing at the door outside. The other disciple, therefore, that was known of the High-priest, went out, and spake unto the portress, and brought in Peter.
text.KJV: 18:16 But Peter stood at the doore without. Then went out that other disciple, which was knowen vnto the high Priest, and spake vnto her that kept the doore, and brought in Peter.

text.NRSV: The woman said to Peter, “You are not also one of this man’s disciples, are you?” He said, “I am not.”
text.Rotherham: The female servant, therefore, the portress, saith unto Peter—Art, thou also, from among the disciples of this man? He, saith—I am not!
text.KJV: 18:17 Then saith the damosell that kept the doore vnto Peter, Art not thou also one of this mans disciples? He sayth, I am not.

text.NRSV: Now the slaves and the police had made a charcoal fire because it was cold, and they were standing around it and warming themselves. Peter also was standing with them and warming himself.
text.Rotherham: Now the servants and the officers were standing by, having made, a coal fire, because it was cold,—and were warming themselves; and Peter also was with them, standing and warming himself.
text.KJV: 18:18 And the seruants and officers stood there, who had made a fire of coales, (for it was colde) and they warmed themselues: and Peter stood with them, and warmed himselfe.

text.NRSV: Then the high priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and about his teaching.
text.Rotherham: The High-priest, therefore, questioned Jesus concerning his disciples, and concerning his teaching.
text.KJV: 18:19 ¶ The high Priest then asked Iesus of his disciples, and of his doctrine.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “I have spoken openly to the world; I have always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews come together. I have said nothing in secret.
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered him—I, openly, have spoken, unto the world,—I, ever, taught, in synagogue, and in the temple, where all the Jews gather together; and, in secret, spake nothing:
text.KJV: 18:20 Iesus answered him, I spake openly to the world, I euer taught in the Synagogue, and in the Temple, whither the Iewes alwayes resort, and in secret haue I said nothing:

text.NRSV: Why do you ask me? Ask those who heard what I said to them; they know what I said.”
text.Rotherham: Why questionest thou me? question them who have heard, what I spake unto them. See! these, know what, I, said.
text.KJV: 18:21 Why askest thou me? Aske them which heard me, what I haue said vnto them: behold, they know what I said.

text.NRSV: When he had said this, one of the police standing nearby struck Jesus on the face, saying, “Is that how you answer the high priest?”
text.Rotherham: And, when, these things, he had said, one of the officers who was standing by, gave a smart blow to Jesus, saying—Thus, answerest thou, the High-priest?
text.KJV: 18:22 And when hee had thus spoken, one of the officers which stood by, stroke Iesus with the palme of his hand, saying, Answerest thou the hie priest so?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “If I have spoken wrongly, testify to the wrong. But if I have spoken rightly, why do you strike me?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered him—If, with abuse, I spake, bear witness of the abuse: but, if with respect, why, smitest thou me?
text.KJV: 18:23 Iesus answered him, If I haue spoken euill, beare witnesse of the euill: but if well, why smitest thou me?

text.NRSV: Then Annas sent him bound to Caiaphas the high priest.
text.Rotherham: Annas therefore sent him forth, bound, unto Caiaphas the High-priest.
text.KJV: 18:24 Now Annas had sent him bound vnto Caiaphas the high Priest.

text.NRSV: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. They asked him, “You are not also one of his disciples, are you?” He denied it and said, “I am not.”
text.Rotherham: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. They said, therefore, unto him—Art, thou also, from among his disciples? He, denied, and said—I am not!
text.KJV: 18:25 And Simon Peter stood and warmed himselfe: They said therefore vnto him, Art not thou also one of his disciples? Hee denied it, and said, I am not.

text.NRSV: One of the slaves of the high priest, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, asked, “Did I not see you in the garden with him?”
text.Rotherham: Saith one from among the servants of the High-priest, being, kinsman, of him whose ear Peter cut off,—Did not, I, see thee, in the garden with him?
text.KJV: 18:26 One of the seruants of the high Priests (being his kinsman whose eare Peter cut off) saith, Did not I see thee in the garden with him?

text.NRSV: Again Peter denied it, and at that moment the cock crowed.
text.Rotherham: Again, therefore, Peter denied. And, straightway, a cock crew.
text.KJV: 18:27 Peter then denied againe, and immediatly the cocke crew.

text.NRSV: Then they took Jesus from Caiaphas to Pilate’s headquarters. It was early morning. They themselves did not enter the headquarters, so as to avoid ritual defilement and to be able to eat the Passover.
text.Rotherham: So they lead Jesus from Caiaphas unto the judgment-hall. Now it was early; and, they themselves, entered not into the judgment-hall, that they might not be defiled, but might eat the passover.
text.KJV: 18:28 ¶ Then led they Iesus from Caiaphas vnto the hall of Iudgement: And it was earely, and they themselues went not into the Iudgement hall, lest they should be defiled: but that they might eat the Passeouer.

text.NRSV: So Pilate went out to them and said, “What accusation do you bring against this man?”
text.Rotherham: Pilate, therefore, went forth outside unto them, and saith,—What accusation bring ye against this man?
text.KJV: 18:29 Pilate then went out vnto them, and said, What accusation bring you against this man?

text.NRSV: They answered, “If this man were not a criminal, we would not have handed him over to you.”
text.Rotherham: They answered and said unto him—If this one had not been doing, mischief, unto thee, had we not delivered him up.
text.KJV: 18:30 They answered, & said vnto him, If he were not a malefactor, we would not haue deliuered him vp vnto thee.

text.NRSV: Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves and judge him according to your law.” The Jews replied, “We are not permitted to put anyone to death.”
text.Rotherham: Pilate, therefore, said unto them—Ye, take him, and, according to your law, judge ye him. The Jews said unto him—Unto us, it is not allowed, to kill anyone!—
text.KJV: 18:31 Then saide Pilate vnto them, Take ye him, and iudge him according to your law. The Iewes therefore said vnto him, It is not lawfull for vs to put any man to death:

text.NRSV: (This was to fulfill the word that Jesus had spoken to indicate the kind of death he was to die.)
text.Rotherham: that, the word of Jesus, might be fulfilled, which he spake, signifying, by what manner of death, he was about to die.
text.KJV: 18:32 That the saying of Iesus might be fulfilled, which hee spake, signifying what death he should die.

text.NRSV: Then Pilate entered the headquarters again, summoned Jesus, and asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?”
text.Rotherham: Pilate, therefore, entered again into the judgment-hall; and addressed Jesus, and said unto him—Art, thou, the king of the Jews?
text.KJV: 18:33 Then Pilate entred into the Iudgement hall againe, and called Iesus, and saide vnto him, Art thou the King of the Iewes?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Do you ask this on your own, or did others tell you about me?”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—Of thyself, art, thou, this thing saying; or did, others, tell thee concerning me?
text.KJV: 18:34 Iesus answered him, Sayest thou this thing of thy selfe? or did others tell it thee of me?

text.NRSV: Pilate replied, “I am not a Jew, am I? Your own nation and the chief priests have handed you over to me. What have you done?”
text.Rotherham: Pilate answered—Am, I, a Jew? Thine own nation, and the High-priests, delivered thee up, unto me! What, hast thou done?
text.KJV: 18:35 Pilate answered, Am I a Iew? Thine owne nation, and the chiefe Priests haue deliuered thee vnto mee: What hast thou done?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “My kingdom is not from this world. If my kingdom were from this world, my followers would be fighting to keep me from being handed over to the Jews. But as it is, my kingdom is not from here.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—My kingdom, is not of this world: If, of this world, had been my kingdom, mine own officers, would have been striving, that I should not be delivered up unto the Jews: but, now, my kingdom, is not from hence.
text.KJV: 18:36 Iesus answered, My kingdome is not of this world: if my kingdome were of this world, then would my seruants fight, that I should not be deliuered to the Iewes: but now is my kingdome not from hence.

text.NRSV: Pilate asked him, “So you are a king?” Jesus answered, “You say that I am a king. For this I was born, and for this I came into the world, to testify to the truth. Everyone who belongs to the truth listens to my voice.”
text.Rotherham: Pilate, therefore, said unto him—And yet, thou, art, not a king? Jesus answered—Thou, sayest, that, a king, I am: I, for this, have been born, and, for this, have come into the world,—that I may bear witness unto the truth: Every one who is of the truth, hearkeneth unto my voice.
text.KJV: 18:37 Pilate therefore saide vnto him, Art thou a King then? Iesus answered, Thou saiest that I am a King. To this end was I borne, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should beare witnesse vnto the trueth: euery one that is of the trueth heareth my voice.

text.NRSV: Pilate asked him, “What is truth?” After he had said this, he went out to the Jews again and told them, “I find no case against him.
text.Rotherham: Pilate saith unto him—What, is truth? And, this saying, again, went he out to the Jews, and saith unto them,—I, find in him, not a single fault.
text.KJV: 18:38 Pilate saith vnto him, What is trueth? And when hee had said this, he went out againe vnto the Iewes, and saith vnto them, I find in him no fault at all.

text.NRSV: But you have a custom that I release someone for you at the Passover. Do you want me to release for you the King of the Jews?”
text.Rotherham: Howbeit ye have, a custom, that, some one, I should release unto you, during the passover:—Are ye minded, then, that I release unto you the king of the Jews?
text.KJV: 18:39 But yee haue a custome that I should release vnto you one at the Passeouer: will ye therefore that I release vnto you the king of the Iewes?

text.NRSV: They shouted in reply, “Not this man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a bandit.
text.Rotherham: So they cried aloud again, saying—Not this man, but Barabbas! Now, Barabbas, was, a robber.
text.KJV: 18:40 Then cried they all againe, saying, Not this man, but Barabbas. Now Barabbas was a robber.

text.NRSV: Then Pilate took Jesus and had him flogged.
text.Rotherham: Then Pilate, therefore, took Jesus, and scourged him.
text.KJV: 19:1 Then Pilate therfore tooke Iesus, and scourged him.

text.NRSV: And the soldiers wove a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and they dressed him in a purple robe.
text.Rotherham: And, the soldiers, plaiting a crown out of thorns, placed it upon his head, and, a purple robe, cast they about him;
text.KJV: 19:2 And the souldiers platted a crowne of thornes, and put it on his head, and they put on him a purple robe,

text.NRSV: They kept coming up to him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!” and striking him on the face.
text.Rotherham: and kept coming unto him, and saying—Joy to thee! O King of the Jews!—and were giving unto him smart blows.
text.KJV: 19:3 And said, Haile king of the Iewes: and they smote him with their hands.

text.NRSV: Pilate went out again and said to them, “Look, I am bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no case against him.”
text.Rotherham: And Pilate went forth again outside, and saith unto them—See! I lead him unto you outside, that ye may take knowledge, that, no single fault, do I find in him.
text.KJV: 19:4 Pilate therefore went foorth againe, and saith vnto them, Behold, I bring him foorth to you, that yee may know that I find no fault in him.

text.NRSV: So Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said to them, “Here is the man!”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, came forth outside, wearing the thorn crown, and the purple mantle. And he saith unto them—Lo! the Man!
text.KJV: 19:5 Then came Iesus forth, wearing the crowne of thornes, and the purple robe: and Pilate saith vnto them, Behold the man.

text.NRSV: When the chief priests and the police saw him, they shouted, “Crucify him! Crucify him!” Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves and crucify him; I find no case against him.”
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, the High-priests and the officers saw him, they cried aloud, saying—Crucify! Crucify! Pilate saith unto them—Ye, take him, and crucify; for, I, find not in him, a fault.
text.KJV: 19:6 When the chiefe Priests therefore and officers saw him, they cried out, saying, Crucifie him, crucifie him. Pilate saith vnto them, Take ye him, and crucifie him: for I find no fault in him.

text.NRSV: The Jews answered him, “We have a law, and according to that law he ought to die because he has claimed to be the Son of God.”
text.Rotherham: The Jews answered him—We, have, a law, and, according to the law, he ought to die, because, Son of God, himself, he made.
text.KJV: 19:7 The Iewes answered him, We haue a law, and by our law he ought to die, because hee made himselfe the Son of God.

text.NRSV: Now when Pilate heard this, he was more afraid than ever.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, Pilate heard this word, he was the more afraid;
text.KJV: 19:8 ¶ When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he was the more afraid,

text.NRSV: He entered his headquarters again and asked Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus gave him no answer.
text.Rotherham: and entered into the judgment-hall again, and saith unto Jesus—Whence, art, thou? But, Jesus, gave him no, answer.
text.KJV: 19:9 And went againe into the iudgement hall, & saith vnto Iesus, Whence art thou? But Iesus gaue him no answere.

text.NRSV: Pilate therefore said to him, “Do you refuse to speak to me? Do you not know that I have power to release you, and power to crucify you?”
text.Rotherham: Pilate, therefore, saith unto him—Unto me, dost thou not speak? Knowest thou not, that, authority, have I to release thee, and, authority, have I to crucify thee?
text.KJV: 19:10 Then saith Pilate vnto him, Speakest thou not vnto me? Knowest thou not, that I haue power to crucifie thee, and haue power to release thee?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered him, “You would have no power over me unless it had been given you from above; therefore the one who handed me over to you is guilty of a greater sin.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered him—Thou couldst have had no authority against me, at all, if it had not been given unto thee from above. Therefore, he that delivered me unto thee, hath, greater sin.
text.KJV: 19:11 Iesus answered, Thou couldest haue no power at all against me, except it were giuen thee from aboue: therfore he that deliuered me vnto thee, hath the greater sinne.

text.NRSV: From then on Pilate tried to release him, but the Jews cried out, “If you release this man, you are no friend of the emperor. Everyone who claims to be a king sets himself against the emperor.”
text.Rotherham: For this cause, Pilate, began seeking to release him; but, the Jews, cried aloud saying—If this man thou release, thou art not a friend of Caesar, for, every one who maketh himself king, speaketh against Caesar.
text.KJV: 19:12 And from thencefore Pilate sought to release him: but the Iewes cried out, saying, If thou let this man goe, thou art not Cesars friend: whosoeuer maketh himselfe a king, speaketh against Cesar.

text.NRSV: When Pilate heard these words, he brought Jesus outside and sat on the judge’s bench at a place called The Stone Pavement, or in Hebrew Gabbatha.
text.Rotherham: Pilate, therefore, when he heard these words, led Jesus outside, and sat down upon a raised seat, in a place called Pavement, but, in Hebrew, Gabbatha.
text.KJV: 19:13 ¶ When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he brought Iesus foorth, and sate downe in the iudgement seate, in a place that is called the pauement, but in the Hebrew, Gabbatha.

text.NRSV: Now it was the day of Preparation for the Passover; and it was about noon. He said to the Jews, “Here is your King!”
text.Rotherham: Now it was the preparation of the passover,—it was about the sixth hour. And he saith unto the Jews—See! your King!
text.KJV: 19:14 And it was the preparation of the Passeouer, and about the sixt houre: and he saith vnto the Iewes, Beholde your King.

text.NRSV: They cried out, “Away with him! Away with him! Crucify him!” Pilate asked them, “Shall I crucify your King?” The chief priests answered, “We have no king but the emperor.”
text.Rotherham: They, therefore, cried aloud—Away! away! Crucify him! Pilate saith unto them—Your king, shall I crucify? The High-priests answered—We have no king but Caesar!
text.KJV: 19:15 But they cried out, Away with him, away with him, crucifie him. Pilate saith vnto them, Shall I crucifie your King? The chiefe Priests answered, Wee haue no king but Cesar.

text.NRSV: Then he handed him over to them to be crucified. So they took Jesus;
text.Rotherham: Then, therefore, he delivered him up unto them, that he might be crucified. They took possession, therefore, of Jesus.
text.KJV: 19:16 Then deliuered he him therfore vnto them to be crucified: and they took Iesus, and led him away.

text.NRSV: and carrying the cross by himself, he went out to what is called The Place of the Skull, which in Hebrew is called Golgotha.
text.Rotherham: And, bearing for himself the cross, he went forth unto the so-called Skull-place, which is named, in Hebrew, Golgotha;
text.KJV: 19:17 And he bearing his crosse, went foorth into a place called the place of a skull, which is called in the Hebrewe, Golgotha:

text.NRSV: There they crucified him, and with him two others, one on either side, with Jesus between them.
text.Rotherham: where, him, they crucified; and, with him, other two, on this side and on that, and, in the midst, Jesus.
text.KJV: 19:18 Where they crucified him, and two other with him, on either side one, and Iesus in the middest.

text.NRSV: Pilate also had an inscription written and put on the cross. It read, “Jesus of Nazareth, the King of the Jews.”
text.Rotherham: And Pilate wrote a title also, and placed on the cross; and there was written—JESUS, THE NAZARENE, THE KING OF THE JEWS.
text.KJV: 19:19 ¶ And Pilate wrote a title, and put it on the crosse. And the writing was, IESVS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE IEWES.

text.NRSV: Many of the Jews read this inscription, because the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city; and it was written in Hebrew, in Latin, and in Greek.
text.Rotherham: This title, therefore, read many of the Jews, because, near, was the place to the city where Jesus was crucified; and it was written in Hebrew, in Latin, in Greek.
text.KJV: 19:20 This title then read many of the Iewes: for the place where Iesus was crucified, was nigh to the citie, and it was written in Hebrewe, and Greeke, and Latine.

text.NRSV: Then the chief priests of the Jews said to Pilate, “Do not write, ‘The King of the Jews,’ but, ‘This man said, I am King of the Jews.’”
text.Rotherham: The High-priests of the Jews, therefore, were saying unto Pilate—Do not be writing, The King of the Jews; but that, he, said: King of the Jews, I am.
text.KJV: 19:21 Then said the chiefe Priests of the Iewes to Pilate, Write not, The king of the Iewes: but that he said, I am King of the Iewes.

text.NRSV: Pilate answered, “What I have written I have written.”
text.Rotherham: Pilate answered—What I have written, I have written!
text.KJV: 19:22 Pilate answered, What I haue written, I haue written.

text.NRSV: When the soldiers had crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and divided them into four parts, one for each soldier. They also took his tunic; now the tunic was seamless, woven in one piece from the top.
text.Rotherham: The soldiers, therefore, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments and made four parts, unto each soldier, a part; also the tunic. Howbeit, the tunic was without seam, from above, woven throughout.
text.KJV: 19:23 ¶ Then the souldiers, when they had crucified Iesus, tooke his garments, (and made foure parts, to euery souldier a part) and also his coat: Now the coate was without seame, wouen from the top thorowout.

text.NRSV: So they said to one another, “Let us not tear it, but cast lots for it to see who will get it.” This was to fulfill what the scripture says, “They divided my clothes among themselves, and for my clothing they cast lots.” And that is what the soldiers did.
text.Rotherham: They said, therefore, one to another—Let us not rend it, but cast lots for it, whose, it shall be;—that, the Scripture, might be fulfilled—They parted my garments amongst them, and, for my vestment, they cast lots:—yes verily, the soldiers, these things did.
text.KJV: 19:24 They said therefore among themselues, Let not vs rent it, but cast lots for it, whose it shall bee: that the Scripture might bee fulfilled, which saith, They parted my raiment among them, and for my vesture they did cast lots. These things therefore the souldiers did.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile, standing near the cross of Jesus were his mother, and his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene.
text.Rotherham: And there were standing by the cross of Jesus, his mother, and his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary the Magdalene.
text.KJV: 19:25 ¶ Now there stood by the crosse of Iesus, his mother, and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Cleophas, and Mary Magdalene.

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing beside her, he said to his mother, “Woman, here is your son.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, seeing his mother and the disciple whom he loved, saith unto his mother—O woman, see! thy son!
text.KJV: 19:26 When Iesus therefore saw his mother, and the disciple standing by, whom he loued, he saith vnto his mother, Woman, behold thy sonne.

text.NRSV: Then he said to the disciple, “Here is your mother.” And from that hour the disciple took her into his own home.
text.Rotherham: Afterwards, he saith unto the disciple—See! thy mother! And, from that hour, the disciple took her unto his own home.
text.KJV: 19:27 Then saith he to the disciple, Behold thy mother. And from that houre that disciple tooke her vnto his owne home.

text.NRSV: After this, when Jesus knew that all was now finished, he said (in order to fulfill the scripture), “I am thirsty.”
text.Rotherham: After this, Jesus, knowing that, already, all things, have been finished,—that the Scripture might be fulfilled, saith—I thirst!
text.KJV: 19:28 ¶ After this, Iesus knowing that all things were now accomplished, that the Scripture might be fulfilled, saith, I thirst.

text.NRSV: A jar full of sour wine was standing there. So they put a sponge full of the wine on a branch of hyssop and held it to his mouth.
text.Rotherham: A vessel, was standing, full of vinegar. A sponge, therefore, full of the vinegar, put about, hyssop, brought they unto his mouth.
text.KJV: 19:29 Now there was set a vessell, full of vineger: And they filled a spunge with vineger, and put it vpon hyssope, and put it to his mouth.

text.NRSV: When Jesus had received the wine, he said, “It is finished.” Then he bowed his head and gave up his spirit.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, he had received the vinegar, Jesus said—It is finished! And, bowing his head, delivered up his spirit.
text.KJV: 19:30 When Iesus therefore had receiued the vineger, he said, It is finished, and he bowed his head, and gaue vp the ghost.

text.NRSV: Since it was the day of Preparation, the Jews did not want the bodies left on the cross during the sabbath, especially because that sabbath was a day of great solemnity. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of the crucified men broken and the bodies removed.
text.Rotherham: The Jews, therefore, since it was, a preparation, that the bodies might not remain upon the cross during the Sabbath,—for that Sabbath day was, great, requested Pilate that their legs might be broken, and they be taken away.
text.KJV: 19:31 The Iewes therefore, because it was the preparation, that the bodies should not remaine vpon the Crosse on the Sabbath day (for that Sabbath day was an high day) besought Pilate that their legs might be broken, and that they might be taken away.

text.NRSV: The soldiers came and broke the legs of the first and of the other who had been crucified with him.
text.Rotherham: The soldiers, therefore, came; and, of the first, indeed, brake the legs, and of the other who was crucified with him,—
text.KJV: 19:32 Then came the souldiers, and brake the legs of the first, and of the other, which was crucified with him.

text.NRSV: But when they came to Jesus and saw that he was already dead, they did not break his legs.
text.Rotherham: but coming, unto Jesus, when they saw that, already, he was dead, they brake not his legs;—
text.KJV: 19:33 But when they came to Iesus, and saw that he was dead already, they brake not his legs.

text.NRSV: Instead, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and at once blood and water came out.
text.Rotherham: but, one of the soldiers, with a spear, pierced, his side, and there came out, straightway, blood and water.
text.KJV: 19:34 But one of the souldiers with a speare pierced his side, and forthwith came there out blood and water.

text.NRSV: (He who saw this has testified so that you also may believe. His testimony is true, and he knows that he tells the truth.)
text.Rotherham: And, he that hath seen, hath borne witness; and, genuine, is his testimony, and, he, knoweth that he saith, what is true, that, ye also, may believe.
text.KJV: 19:35 And he that saw it, bare record, and his record is true, and he knoweth that hee saith true, that yee might beleeue.

text.NRSV: These things occurred so that the scripture might be fulfilled, “None of his bones shall be broken.”
text.Rotherham: For these things came to pass, that, the Scripture, might be fulfilled—A bone thereof, shall not be crushed;
text.KJV: 19:36 For these things were done, that the Scripture should be fulfilled, A bone of him shall not be broken.

text.NRSV: And again another passage of scripture says, “They will look on the one whom they have pierced.”
text.Rotherham: and, again, a different Scripture, saith—They shall look unto him whom they pierced.
text.KJV: 19:37 And againe another Scripture saith, They shall looke on him whom they piersed.

text.NRSV: After these things, Joseph of Arimathea, who was a disciple of Jesus, though a secret one because of his fear of the Jews, asked Pilate to let him take away the body of Jesus. Pilate gave him permission; so he came and removed his body.
text.Rotherham: But, after these things, Joseph from Arimathaea, being a disciple of Jesus, but having kept it secret for fear of the Jews, requested Pilate, that he might take away the body of Jesus; and Pilate gave permission. He came, therefore, and took away his body.
text.KJV: 19:38 ¶ And after this, Ioseph of Arimathea (being a disciple of Iesus, but secretly for feare of the Iewes) besought Pilate that he might take away the body of Iesus, and Pilate gaue him leaue: he came therefore, and tooke the body of Iesus.

text.NRSV: Nicodemus, who had at first come to Jesus by night, also came, bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, weighing about a hundred pounds.
text.Rotherham: There came, moreover, Nicodemus also,—he that came unto him by night at the first,—bearing a roll of myrrh and aloes, about a hundred pounds’ weight.
text.KJV: 19:39 And there came also Nicodemus, which at the first came to Iesus by night, and brought a mixture of myrrhe and aloes, about an hundred pound weight.

text.NRSV: They took the body of Jesus and wrapped it with the spices in linen cloths, according to the burial custom of the Jews.
text.Rotherham: So they received the body of Jesus, and bound it in linen-bandages with the spices,—just as it is, a custom, with the Jews to prepare for burial.
text.KJV: 19:40 Then tooke they the body of Iesus, & wound it in linnen clothes, with the spices, as the maner of the Iewes is to burie:

text.NRSV: Now there was a garden in the place where he was crucified, and in the garden there was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
text.Rotherham: Now there was, in the place where he was crucified, a garden; and, in the garden, an unused tomb, wherein, as yet, no one had been laid.
text.KJV: 19:41 Now in the place where he was crucified, there was a garden, and in the garden a new Sepulchre, wherein was neuer man yet layd.

text.NRSV: And so, because it was the Jewish day of Preparation, and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus there.
text.Rotherham: So, there, by reason of the preparation of the Jews, because, near, was the tomb, laid they Jesus.
text.KJV: 19:42 There laid they Iesus therefore, because of the Iewes preparation day, for the Sepulchre was nigh at hand.

text.NRSV: Early on the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been removed from the tomb.
text.Rotherham: But, on the first day of the week, Mary the Magdalene, cometh early, while it is yet, dark, unto the tomb,—and beholdeth the stone, already taken away out of the tomb.
text.KJV: 20:1 The first day of the weeke, commeth Mary Magdalene earely when it was yet darke, vnto the Sepulchre, and seeth the stone taken away from the Sepulchre.

text.NRSV: So she ran and went to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken the Lord out of the tomb, and we do not know where they have laid him.”
text.Rotherham: She runneth, therefore, and cometh unto Simon Peter, and unto the other disciple whom Jesus dearly loved, and saith unto them—They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we know not where they have laid him.
text.KJV: 20:2 Then she runneth and commeth to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple whom Iesus loued, and saith vnto them, They haue taken away the Lord out of the Sepulchre, and we know not where they haue laid him.

text.NRSV: Then Peter and the other disciple set out and went toward the tomb.
text.Rotherham: Peter, therefore, went out, and the other disciple,—and they were going unto the tomb;
text.KJV: 20:3 Peter therefore went forth, and that other disciple, and came to the Sepulchre.

text.NRSV: The two were running together, but the other disciple outran Peter and reached the tomb first.
text.Rotherham: and the two were running together, and, the other disciple, outran Peter, and came first unto the tomb;
text.KJV: 20:4 So they ranne both together, and the other disciple did outrun Peter, and came first to the Sepulchre.

text.NRSV: He bent down to look in and saw the linen cloths lying there, but he did not go in.
text.Rotherham: and, stooping aside, beholdeth, lying, the linen-bandages, nevertheless, he entered not.
text.KJV: 20:5 And he stouping downe and looking in, saw the linnen clothes lying, yet went he not in.

text.NRSV: Then Simon Peter came, following him, and went into the tomb. He saw the linen cloths lying there,
text.Rotherham: So Simon Peter also cometh, following him, and entered into the tomb, and vieweth the linen-bandages lying,—
text.KJV: 20:6 Then commeth Simon Peter following him, and went into the Sepulchre, and seeth the linnen clothes lie,

text.NRSV: and the cloth that had been on Jesus’ head, not lying with the linen cloths but rolled up in a place by itself.
text.Rotherham: and the napkin, which was upon his head, not, with the linen-bandages, lying, but apart, folded up into one place.
text.KJV: 20:7 And the napkin that was about his head, not lying with the linnen clothes, but wrapped together in a place by it selfe.

text.NRSV: Then the other disciple, who reached the tomb first, also went in, and he saw and believed;
text.Rotherham: Then, entered, therefore, the other disciple also, who had come first unto the tomb, and he saw and believed.
text.KJV: 20:8 Then went in also that other disciple which came first to the Sepulchre, and he saw, and beleeued.

text.NRSV: for as yet they did not understand the scripture, that he must rise from the dead.
text.Rotherham: For, not yet, knew they the Scripture, that he must needs, from among the dead, arise.
text.KJV: 20:9 For as yet they knew not the Scripture, that hee must rise againe from the dead.

text.NRSV: Then the disciples returned to their homes.
text.Rotherham: The disciples, therefore, departed again, by themselves.
text.KJV: 20:10 Then the disciples went away againe vnto their owne home.

text.NRSV: But Mary stood weeping outside the tomb. As she wept, she bent over to look into the tomb;
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, Mary, remained standing against the tomb, outside, weeping. So then, as she wept, she stooped aside into the tomb,
text.KJV: 20:11 ¶ But Mary stood without at the sepulchre, weeping: & as shee wept, she stouped downe, and looked into the Sepulchre,

text.NRSV: and she saw two angels in white, sitting where the body of Jesus had been lying, one at the head and the other at the feet.
text.Rotherham: and beholdeth two messengers, in white garments, sitting, one at the head, and the other at the feet, where had been lying the body of Jesus.
text.KJV: 20:12 And seeth two Angels in white, sitting, the one at the head, and the other at the feete, where the body of Iesus had layen:

text.NRSV: They said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” She said to them, “They have taken away my Lord, and I do not know where they have laid him.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, say unto her—Woman! why weepest thou? She saith unto them—They have taken away my Lord, and I know not where they have laid him.
text.KJV: 20:13 And they say vnto her, Woman, why weepest thou? Shee saith vnto them, Because they haue taken away my Lord, and I know not where they haue laied him.

text.NRSV: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she did not know that it was Jesus.
text.Rotherham: These things saying, she turned round, and seeth Jesus standing, and knew not that it was, Jesus.
text.KJV: 20:14 And when she had thus said, she turned herselfe backe, and saw Iesus standing, and knew not that it was Iesus.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you looking for?” Supposing him to be the gardener, she said to him, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will take him away.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—Woman! why weepest thou? Whom seekest thou? She, supposing he was, the gardener, saith unto him—Sir! If, thou, hast borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him; and, I, will take him away.
text.KJV: 20:15 Iesus saith vnto her, Woman, why weepest thou? whom seekest thou? She supposing him to be the gardiner, saith vnto him, Sir, if thou haue borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laied him, and I will take him away.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Mary!” She turned and said to him in Hebrew, “Rabbouni!” (which means Teacher).
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—Mary! She, turning saith unto him, in Hebrew—Rabboni! which meaneth, Teacher.
text.KJV: 20:16 Iesus saith vnto her, Mary. She turned herselfe, and saith vnto him, Rabboni, which is to say, Master.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to her, “Do not hold on to me, because I have not yet ascended to the Father. But go to my brothers and say to them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto her—Be not detaining me, for, not yet, have I ascended unto the Father; but be going unto my disciples, and say unto them—I am ascending unto my Father and your Father, and my God and your God.
text.KJV: 20:17 Iesus saith vnto her, Touch me not: for I am not yet ascended to my Father: but goe to my brethren, and say vnto them, I ascend vnto my Father, and your Father, and to my God, and your God.

text.NRSV: Mary Magdalene went and announced to the disciples, “I have seen the Lord”; and she told them that he had said these things to her.
text.Rotherham: Mary the Magdalene cometh, bringing tidings unto the disciples—I have seen the Lord! and that these things he had said unto her.
text.KJV: 20:18 Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that shee had seene the Lord, and that hee had spoken these things vnto her.

text.NRSV: When it was evening on that day, the first day of the week, and the doors of the house where the disciples had met were locked for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you.”
text.Rotherham: It being late, therefore, on that day, the first of the week,—and, the doors, having been made fast where the disciples were, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came, and stood in the midst, and saith unto them—Peace be unto you!
text.KJV: 20:19 ¶ Then the same day at euening, being the first day of the weeke, when the doores were shut, where the disciples were assembled for feare of the Iewes, came Iesus, and stood in the midst, and saith vnto them, Peace bee vnto you.

text.NRSV: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. Then the disciples rejoiced when they saw the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and, this, saying he pointed out both his hands and his side unto them. The disciples, therefore, rejoiced, seeing the Lord.
text.KJV: 20:20 And when hee had so saide, hee shewed vnto them his hands and his side. Then were the disciples glad, when they saw the Lord.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them again, “Peace be with you. As the Father has sent me, so I send you.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus, therefore, said unto them, again,—Peace be unto you! Just as, my Father, sent me forth, I, also, send you.
text.KJV: 20:21 Then said Iesus to them againe, Peace be vnto you: As my Father hath sent me, euen so send I you.

text.NRSV: When he had said this, he breathed on them and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: And, this, saying, he breathed strongly, and saith unto them—Receive ye Holy Spirit:—
text.KJV: 20:22 And when he had said this, hee breathed on them, and saith vnto them, Receiue ye the holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: If you forgive the sins of any, they are forgiven them; if you retain the sins of any, they are retained.”
text.Rotherham: Whosesoever sins ye shall remit, they are remitted unto them, whosesoever ye shall retain, they are retained.
text.KJV: 20:23 Whose soeuer sinnes yee remit, they are remitted vnto them, and whose soeuer sinnes yee retaine, they are retained.

text.NRSV: But Thomas (who was called the Twin), one of the twelve, was not with them when Jesus came.
text.Rotherham: But, Thomas, one of the twelve, the one called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.
text.KJV: 20:24 ¶ But Thomas one of the twelue, called Didymus, was not with them when Iesus came.

text.NRSV: So the other disciples told him, “We have seen the Lord.” But he said to them, “Unless I see the mark of the nails in his hands, and put my finger in the mark of the nails and my hand in his side, I will not believe.”
text.Rotherham: The other disciples, therefore, were saying unto him—We have seen the Lord! But, he, said unto them—Except I see, in his hands, the print of the nails, and press my finger into the print of the nails, and press my hand into his side, in nowise will I believe.
text.KJV: 20:25 The other disciples therefore said vnto him, We haue seene the Lord. But he said vnto them, Except I shall see in his hands the print of the nailes, and put my finger into the print of the nailes, and thrust my hand into his side, I will not beleeue.

text.NRSV: A week later his disciples were again in the house, and Thomas was with them. Although the doors were shut, Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you.”
text.Rotherham: And, eight days after, his disciples again were within, and Thomas with them. Jesus cometh—the doors having been made fast—and stood in the midst, and said—Peace be unto you!
text.KJV: 20:26 ¶ And after eight dayes, againe his disciples were within, and Thomas with them: Then came Iesus, the doores being shut, and stood in the midst, and said, Peace be vnto you.

text.NRSV: Then he said to Thomas, “Put your finger here and see my hands. Reach out your hand and put it in my side. Do not doubt but believe.”
